8	/-!
9	# Grassmannian affine charts
10	
11	This file contains the single blueprint-pinned declaration for the affine
12	charts of the Grassmannian scheme: `AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.affineChart`.
13	
14	For `I ⊆ Fin r` with `#I = d`, the affine chart `U^I` of the Grassmannian
15	`Gr(d, r)` is the spectrum of the polynomial ring in the `d(r-d)` free matrix
16	entries — `Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{q ∉ I}` — which is non-canonically isomorphic
17	to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
18	
19	Blueprint reference: `def:gr_affine_chart`
20	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`).
21	-/
22	
23	set_option autoImplicit false
24	
25	universe u
26	
27	open AlgebraicGeometry
28	
29	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian
30	
31	/- Blueprint: def:gr_affine_chart (chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex) -/
32	
33	/- Planner note:
34	   Blueprint `def:gr_affine_chart` (Nitsure §1, "Construction by gluing together
35	   affine patches"): for `I ⊆ {1..r}` with `#I = d`, `X^I` is the `d×r` matrix
36	   whose `I`-minor is the `d×d` identity and whose other `d(r-d)` entries are
37	   independent indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` over ℤ. The affine chart is
38	   `U^I := Spec ℤ[X^I] = Spec (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)` —
39	   the spectrum of the polynomial ring on the `d(r-d)` free entries; non-canonically
40	   `≅ 𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`. The prover should build `affineChart` as
41	   `AlgebraicGeometry.Spec` of that `CommRingCat` (the MvPolynomial ring on the
42	   free-entry index type), via
43	   `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` or an equivalent index of cardinality
44	   `d(r-d)`. -/
45	
46	/-- The **affine chart** `U^I` of the Grassmannian `Gr(d, r)` indexed by a
47	`d`-element subset `I : Finset (Fin r)`.
48	
49	Concretely `U^I = Spec ℤ[x^I_{p,q}]_{p : Fin d, q ∉ I}`, the spectrum of the
50	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ` in the `d(r-d)`
51	free entries of the standard matrix representative with `I`-minor equal to the
52	identity. This chart is non-canonically isomorphic to `𝔸^{d(r-d)}_ℤ`.
53	
54	The Grassmannian scheme is obtained by gluing the `Nat.choose r d` affine charts
55	`U^I` along the Plücker cocycle transition maps. -/
56	noncomputable def affineChart (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) : Scheme :=
57	  AlgebraicGeometry.Spec (CommRingCat.of (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ))
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Grassmannian transition maps
60	
61	The transition map `θ_{I,J}` of `def:gr_transition` is a Cramer-inverse computation
62	over the localised chart ring `R^I_J := ℤ[X^I, 1/P^I_J]`, followed by the universal
63	property of the away-localisation. We build it bottom-up over the chart ring
64	`R^I := MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ`, all elementary matrix algebra
65	over `ℤ`. The `d`-subset combinatorics are handled by the order isomorphism
66	`Finset.orderIsoOfFin : I.card = d → (Fin d ≃o ↥I)`.
67	
68	Blueprint references: `def:gr_universal_matrix`, `def:gr_minor_det`,
69	`def:gr_universal_minor`, `lem:gr_minorDet_unit`, `def:gr_universalMinorInv`,
70	`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`, `def:gr_image_matrix`, `def:gr_transition_pre`
71	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
72	
73	/-- The **universal matrix** `X^I` (`def:gr_universal_matrix`): the `d × r` matrix
74	over the chart ring `R^I = MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q // q ∉ I}) ℤ` whose `I`-minor is the
75	`d × d` identity (read through the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥I`) and whose remaining
76	`d(r-d)` entries are the free indeterminates `x^I_{p,q}` (`q ∉ I`). Project-local:
77	the universal point of the affine chart `affineChart d r I`. -/
78	noncomputable def universalMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
79	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) :=
80	  fun p q =>
81	    if h : q ∈ I then (if (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = q then 1 else 0)
82	    else MvPolynomial.X (p, ⟨q, h⟩)
83	
84	/-- The **minor determinant** `P^I_J = det(X^I_J)` (`def:gr_minor_det`): the determinant
85	of the `d × d` submatrix of `universalMatrix d r I` whose columns are those indexed by
86	`J`, reindexed to `Fin d` via the order iso `Fin d ≃o ↥J`. Project-local: defines the
87	principal open `U^I_J = Spec R^I[1/P^I_J]`. -/
88	noncomputable def minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
89	    (hJ : J.card = d) : MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ :=
90	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
91	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
92	
93	/-- The **localised `J`-minor** `X^I_J` over `R^I_J` (`def:gr_universal_minor`): the
94	`J`-minor of `universalMatrix d r I` with entries pushed forward along the structure map
95	`R^I → R^I_J = Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J)`. Project-local. -/
96	noncomputable def universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
97	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
98	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
99	  ((universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
100	    (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).map (algebraMap _ _)
101	
102	/-- The localised minor determinant is a unit (`lem:gr_minorDet_unit`): `det(X^I_J)` is the
103	image of `P^I_J` under the away-localisation structure map, hence a unit. Project-local. -/
104	theorem isUnit_det_universalMinor (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
105	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det := by
106	  have h : (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det
107	      = (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ))) (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
108	    (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
109	  rw [h]
110	  exact IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
111	
112	/-- The **Cramer inverse** `(X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_universalMinorInv`): the Mathlib
113	nonsingular inverse of the localised `J`-minor. Its entries lie in `R^I_J` by Cramer's
114	rule. Project-local. -/
115	noncomputable def universalMinorInv (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
116	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
117	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
118	  (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⁻¹
119	
120	/-- The Cramer inverse is a two-sided inverse (`lem:gr_universalMinorInv_identities`):
121	since `det(X^I_J)` is a unit, `(X^I_J)⁻¹` is a genuine left and right inverse.
122	Project-local. -/
123	theorem universalMinorInv_mul_cancel (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
124	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
125	    universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * universalMinor d r I J hI hJ = 1 ∧
126	    universalMinor d r I J hI hJ * universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ = 1 :=
127	  ⟨Matrix.nonsing_inv_mul _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ),
128	   Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv _ (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I J hI hJ)⟩
129	
130	/-- The **image matrix** `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` (`def:gr_image_matrix`): the product of the
131	Cramer inverse with the universal matrix base-changed to `R^I_J`. Its entries are the
132	prospective images of the indeterminates `x^J_{p,q}` under `θ_{I,J}`. Project-local. -/
133	noncomputable def imageMatrix (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
134	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
135	    Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ)) :=
136	  universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ * (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)
137	
138	/-- The **pre-localisation hom** `θ̃_{I,J} : R^J → R^I_J` (`def:gr_transition_pre`): the
139	`ℤ`-algebra map out of the chart ring of `J` sending each free indeterminate `x^J_{p,q}`
140	to the `(p,q)`-entry of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`. Project-local. -/
141	noncomputable def transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
142	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
143	    MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ J}) ℤ →ₐ[ℤ]
144	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
145	  MvPolynomial.aeval (fun e => imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ e.1 e.2.1)
146	
147	/-- The `I`-minor of the universal matrix `X^I` is the `d × d` identity (`P^I_I = 1`
148	underlies `lem:gr_transition_self`): the `I`-columns of `X^I` read through the order iso
149	`Fin d ≃o ↥I` form the identity block. Project-local. -/
150	theorem universalMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
151	    (universalMatrix d r I hI).submatrix id
152	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
153	  ext p p'
154	  simp only [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq, universalMatrix]
155	  rw [dif_pos (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p').2, Matrix.one_apply]
156	  have hiff : ((I.orderIsoOfFin hI p : Fin r) = (I.orderIsoOfFin hI p')) ↔ (p = p') := by
157	    rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
158	  by_cases h : p = p'
159	  · rw [if_pos (hiff.mpr h), if_pos h]
160	  · rw [if_neg (hiff.not.mpr h), if_neg h]
161	
162	/-- Submatrix on the columns of a right matrix factor commutes with the product
163	(rows reindexed by `id`). Project-local helper: avoids a matrix-multiplication
164	instance-keying issue that blocks the generic `rw` of Mathlib's submatrix-mul lemmas. -/
165	private lemma mul_submatrix_col {d r : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
166	    (A : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin r) R) (g : Fin d → Fin r) :
167	    (A * B).submatrix id g = A * B.submatrix id g := by
168	  ext i j; simp [Matrix.mul_apply, Matrix.submatrix_apply]
169	
170	/-- The `J`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the identity: `M_J =
171	(X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_J = 1` (`def:gr_image_matrix`, used in the cocycle/transition arguments).
172	Project-local. -/
173	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_self (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
174	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
175	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
176	      (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r)) = 1 := by
177	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
178	        (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))
179	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
180	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
181	          (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
182	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map]
183	  exact (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1
184	
185	/-- The `I`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I` is the Cramer inverse:
186	`M_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I_I = (X^I_J)⁻¹` (`def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
187	theorem imageMatrix_submatrix_I (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
188	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
189	    (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
190	      (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r)) = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ := by
191	  have h1 : (imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
192	        (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))
193	      = universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ *
194	        (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
195	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
196	  rw [h1, Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_submatrix_self,
197	    Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _), mul_one]
198	
199	/-- The pre-localisation hom realises the matrix formula `θ̃_{I,J}(X^J) = (X^I_J)⁻¹ X^I`:
200	applying `θ̃_{I,J}` entrywise to the universal matrix `X^J` yields the image matrix `M`
201	(`def:gr_transition_pre`, `def:gr_image_matrix`). Project-local. -/
202	theorem universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
203	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
204	    (universalMatrix d r J hJ).map (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)
205	      = imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ := by
206	  ext p q
207	  simp only [Matrix.map_apply, universalMatrix]
208	  by_cases hq : q ∈ J
209	  · rw [dif_pos hq]
210	    set k := (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ).symm ⟨q, hq⟩ with hk
211	    have hqk : (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ k : Fin r) = q := by simp [hk]
212	    have himg : imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ p q = (1 : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) _) p k := by
213	      have e := congrFun (congrFun (imageMatrix_submatrix_self d r I J hI hJ) p) k
214	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_apply, id_eq] at e
215	      rw [← hqk]; exact e
216	    rw [himg, Matrix.one_apply, apply_ite (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ), map_one, map_zero]
217	    have hcond : ((J.orderIsoOfFin hJ p : Fin r) = q) ↔ (p = k) := by
218	      conv_lhs => rw [← hqk]
219	      rw [Subtype.coe_inj, EmbeddingLike.apply_eq_iff_eq]
220	    by_cases hpk : p = k
221	    · rw [if_pos (hcond.mpr hpk), if_pos hpk]
222	    · rw [if_neg (hcond.not.mpr hpk), if_neg hpk]
223	  · rw [dif_neg hq, transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X]
224	
225	/-- The pre-hom sends `P^J_I` to a unit (`lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`):
226	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I) = det((X^I_J)⁻¹) = det(X^I_J)⁻¹ = 1/P^I_J`, a unit of `R^I_J`. This is the
227	hypothesis that lets `θ̃_{I,J}` extend along the away-localisation `R^J[1/P^J_I]`.
228	Project-local. -/
229	theorem isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
230	    (hJ : J.card = d) : IsUnit (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)) := by
231	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
232	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
233	          (fun j : Fin d => (I.orderIsoOfFin hI j : Fin r))).map
234	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
235	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
236	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap, imageMatrix_submatrix_I]
237	  have hmul : (universalMinorInv d r I J hI hJ).det * (universalMinor d r I J hI hJ).det = 1 := by
238	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r I J hI hJ).1, Matrix.det_one]
239	  exact IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one _ hmul
240	
241	/-- The **transition map** `θ_{I,J} : R^J[1/P^J_I] → R^I[1/P^I_J]` (`def:gr_transition`):
242	the away-localisation lift of the pre-hom `θ̃_{I,J}` along `P^J_I`, available because
243	`θ̃_{I,J}(P^J_I)` is a unit (`isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet`). It is the comorphism of
244	the chart-overlap isomorphism `U^I_J → U^J_I`. Project-local. -/
245	noncomputable def transitionMap (d r : ℕ) (I J : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
246	    (hJ : J.card = d) :
247	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) →+* Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) :=
248	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI)
249	    (g := (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
250	    (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ)
251	
252	/-- `θ_{I,I}` is the identity (`lem:gr_transition_self`): since `P^I_I = 1` the minor
253	`X^I_I = 1` is its own Cramer inverse, so the pre-hom is the structure map and its
254	away-localisation lift is the identity ring homomorphism. Project-local. -/
255	theorem transitionMap_self (d r : ℕ) (I : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d) :
256	    transitionMap d r I I hI hI = RingHom.id (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
257	  have hmin1 : universalMinor d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
258	    simp only [universalMinor, universalMatrix_submatrix_self]
259	    exact Matrix.map_one _ (map_zero _) (map_one _)
260	  have hinv1 : universalMinorInv d r I I hI hI = 1 := by
261	    simp only [universalMinorInv, hmin1]
262	    exact inv_one
263	  have himg : imageMatrix d r I I hI hI = (universalMatrix d r I hI).map (algebraMap _ _) := by
264	    rw [imageMatrix, hinv1]
265	    exact Matrix.one_mul _
266	  have hpre : (transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI).toRingHom
267	      = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
268	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)) := by
269	    apply MvPolynomial.ringHom_ext
270	    · intro n
271	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.C n) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.C n)
272	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_C,
273	        IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply ℤ (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
274	          (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I I hI hI)), MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
275	    · intro e
276	      change transitionPreMap d r I I hI hI (MvPolynomial.X e) = algebraMap _ _ (MvPolynomial.X e)
277	      rw [transitionPreMap, MvPolynomial.aeval_X, himg, Matrix.map_apply]
278	      congr 1
279	      rw [universalMatrix, dif_neg e.2.2]
280	  apply IsLocalization.ringHom_ext (Submonoid.powers (minorDet d r I I hI hI))
281	  simp only [transitionMap, IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp, RingHom.id_comp]
282	  exact hpre
283	
284	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — triple-overlap rings and the cocycle
285	
286	The cocycle condition `θ_{I,K} = θ_{I,J} ∘ θ_{J,K}` cannot be stated as a naive
287	composition of the landed `transitionMap`s: the codomain of `transitionMap d r J K`
288	(`R^J[1/P^J_K]`) differs from the domain of `transitionMap d r I J` (`R^J[1/P^J_I]`).
289	The identity therefore lives over the *triple-overlap* rings obtained by inverting BOTH
290	relevant minors in each chart ring:
291	`S_K := R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]`, `S_J := R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`, `S_I := R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`.
292	We build the localised transition maps `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`,
293	`Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I` over these rings and verify the cocycle.
294	
295	Blueprint reference: `lem:gr_cocycle` (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_GrassmannianCells.tex`). -/
296	
297	/-- A ring homomorphism carries the nonsingular (Cramer) inverse to the nonsingular
298	inverse, provided the determinant is a unit. Project-local helper: Mathlib has no direct
299	`map`-`nonsingular inverse` compatibility lemma. -/
300	private lemma map_nonsing_inv {n : ℕ} {R S : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing S] (f : R →+* S)
301	    (A : Matrix (Fin n) (Fin n) R) (h : IsUnit A.det) :
302	    (A.map f)⁻¹ = A⁻¹.map f := by
303	  have hmul : (A.map f) * (A⁻¹.map f) = 1 := by
304	    rw [← Matrix.map_mul, Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A h, Matrix.map_one f (map_zero f) (map_one f)]
305	  exact Matrix.inv_eq_right_inv hmul
306	
307	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `x` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
308	(inverting the extra factor `y`). Project-local: the structure map of the triple overlap
309	relative to a double-localisation. -/
310	noncomputable def awayInclLeft {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
311	    Localization.Away x →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
312	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away x) x
313	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
314	    (by
315	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
316	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
317	      rw [map_mul] at h
318	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h)
319	
320	/-- Inclusion of the away-localisation at `y` into the away-localisation at `x * y`
321	(inverting the extra factor `x`). Project-local. -/
322	noncomputable def awayInclRight {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
323	    Localization.Away y →+* Localization.Away (x * y) :=
324	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (S := Localization.Away y) y
325	    (g := algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)))
326	    (by
327	      have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
328	        IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
329	      rw [map_mul] at h
330	      exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_right h)
331	
332	/-- `awayInclLeft` is the canonical map over `R`: it intertwines the two structure maps.
333	Project-local. -/
334	lemma awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
335	    (awayInclLeft x y).comp (algebraMap R (Localization.Away x)) =
336	      algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
337	  rw [awayInclLeft]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp x _
338	
339	/-- `awayInclRight` is the canonical map over `R`: it intertwines the two structure maps.
340	Project-local. -/
341	lemma awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
342	    (awayInclRight x y).comp (algebraMap R (Localization.Away y)) =
343	      algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) := by
344	  rw [awayInclRight]; exact IsLocalization.Away.lift_comp y _
345	
346	/-- The image of a general minor determinant `P^B_C` under the pre-localisation hom
347	`θ̃_{A,B}` is the determinant of the `C`-minor of the image matrix `M = (X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A`
348	(generalises the `C = A` computation underlying `lem:gr_transition_pre_unit`). Project-local. -/
349	theorem transitionPreMap_minorDet (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
350	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
351	    transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
352	      = ((imageMatrix d r I J hI hJ).submatrix id
353	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).det := by
354	  have e1 : transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
355	      = (((universalMatrix d r J hJ).submatrix id
356	          (fun j : Fin d => (K.orderIsoOfFin hK j : Fin r))).map
357	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ)).det :=
358	    RingHom.map_det (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom _
359	  rw [e1, ← Matrix.submatrix_map, universalMatrix_map_transitionPreMap]
360	
361	/-- The "cross" minor `P^B_C` is sent by `θ̃_{A,B}`, then pushed into a double localisation
362	`D` (in which `P^A_C` is a unit), to a unit. Concretely
363	`θ̃_{A,B}(P^B_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹ X^A_C) = det((X^A_B)⁻¹) · P^A_C`, a product of two units
364	once `P^A_C` is inverted. This is the cross-factor input to each triple-overlap transition
365	lift. Project-local. -/
366	private lemma isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross
367	    (d r : ℕ) (A B C : Finset (Fin r)) (hA : A.card = d) (hB : B.card = d) (hC : C.card = d)
368	    {D : Type*} [CommRing D] [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D]
369	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB) →+* D)
370	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
371	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))
372	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D)
373	    (hunit : IsUnit (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D
374	        (minorDet d r A C hA hC))) :
375	    IsUnit (incl (transitionPreMap d r A B hA hB (minorDet d r B C hB hC))) := by
376	  have hsub : (imageMatrix d r A B hA hB).submatrix id
377	        (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))
378	      = universalMinorInv d r A B hA hB *
379	        (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map (algebraMap _ _)).submatrix id
380	          (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))) := mul_submatrix_col _ _ _
381	  rw [transitionPreMap_minorDet, hsub, Matrix.det_mul, map_mul]
382	  refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
383	  · refine IsUnit.map incl ?_
384	    refine IsUnit.of_mul_eq_one (universalMinor d r A B hA hB).det ?_
385	    rw [← Matrix.det_mul, (universalMinorInv_mul_cancel d r A B hA hB).1, Matrix.det_one]
386	  · have hdet : (((universalMatrix d r A hA).map
387	            (algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)))).submatrix id
388	            (fun j : Fin d => (C.orderIsoOfFin hC j : Fin r))).det
389	          = algebraMap _ (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC) := by
390	      rw [Matrix.submatrix_map]
391	      exact (RingHom.map_det _ _).symm
392	    rw [hdet]
393	    have hcomp : incl (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ)
394	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r A B hA hB)) (minorDet d r A C hA hC))
395	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ A}) ℤ) D (minorDet d r A C hA hC) :=
396	      RingHom.congr_fun hincl _
397	    rw [hcomp]
398	    exact hunit
399	
400	/-- The left factor of a product is a unit in the away-localisation at that product.
401	Project-local. -/
402	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_left {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
403	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) x) := by
404	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
405	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
406	  rw [map_mul] at h
407	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_left h
408	
409	/-- The right factor of a product is a unit in the away-localisation at that product.
410	Project-local. -/
411	private lemma isUnit_algebraMap_away_right {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (x y : R) :
412	    IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) y) := by
413	  have h : IsUnit (algebraMap R (Localization.Away (x * y)) (x * y)) :=
414	    IsLocalization.Away.algebraMap_isUnit _
415	  rw [map_mul] at h
416	  exact isUnit_of_mul_isUnit_right h
417	
418	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,J} : S_J →+* S_I`, where
419	`S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`: the away-localisation lift of
420	`θ̃_{I,J}` (post-composed into `S_I`) along the doubly-inverted minor `P^J_I P^J_K`.
421	Project-local. -/
422	noncomputable def cocycleΘIJ (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
423	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
424	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) →+*
425	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
426	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK)
427	    (g := (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
428	            (transitionPreMap d r I J hI hJ).toRingHom)
429	    (by
430	      rw [map_mul]
431	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
432	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I J hI hJ).map
433	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
434	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I J K hI hJ hK
435	          (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
436	          (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
437	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_right _ _))
438	
439	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{J,K} : S_K →+* S_J`, where
440	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_J = R^J[1/(P^J_I P^J_K)]`. Project-local. -/
441	noncomputable def cocycleΘJK (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
442	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
443	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ) →+*
444	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r J I hJ hI * minorDet d r J K hJ hK) :=
445	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)
446	    (g := (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)).comp
447	            (transitionPreMap d r J K hJ hK).toRingHom)
448	    (by
449	      rw [map_mul]
450	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
451	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r J K I hJ hK hI
452	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))
453	          (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
454	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _)
455	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r J K hJ hK).map
456	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK)))
457	
458	/-- The triple-overlap transition map `Θ_{I,K} : S_K →+* S_I`, where
459	`S_K = R^K[1/(P^K_I P^K_J)]` and `S_I = R^I[1/(P^I_J P^I_K)]`. Project-local. -/
460	noncomputable def cocycleΘIK (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
461	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
462	    Localization.Away (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ) →+*
463	      Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK) :=
464	  IsLocalization.Away.lift (minorDet d r K I hK hI * minorDet d r K J hK hJ)
465	    (g := (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK)).comp
466	            (transitionPreMap d r I K hI hK).toRingHom)
467	    (by
468	      rw [map_mul]
469	      refine IsUnit.mul ?_ ?_
470	      · exact (isUnit_transitionPreMap_minorDet d r I K hI hK).map
471	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
472	      · exact isUnit_incl_transitionPreMap_cross d r I K J hI hK hJ
473	          (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
474	          (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
475	          (isUnit_algebraMap_away_left _ _))
476	
477	/-- Mapping a base-changed matrix through a further ring hom collapses to a single base change
478	when the homs compose correctly. Project-local helper. -/
479	private lemma map_map_eq_of_comp {m n : ℕ} {R A D : Type*} [CommRing R] [CommRing A] [CommRing D]
480	    (M : Matrix (Fin m) (Fin n) R) (f : R →+* A) (g : A →+* D) (h : R →+* D)
481	    (hcomp : g.comp f = h) : (M.map f).map g = M.map h := by
482	  rw [Matrix.map_map, ← RingHom.coe_comp, hcomp]
483	
484	/-- Mapping the image matrix `M = (X^I_X)⁻¹ X^I` through any ring hom `incl` lying over the
485	structure map `R^I → D` (i.e. `incl ∘ (R^I → R^I_X) = (R^I → D)`) yields `(Y_X)⁻¹ Y`, where
486	`Y := X^I` base-changed to `D`. The key reusable step in the cocycle computation. Project-local. -/
487	private lemma imageMatrix_map_eq (d r : ℕ) (I X : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
488	    (hX : X.card = d) {D : Type*} [CommRing D]
489	    [Algebra (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D]
490	    (incl : Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX) →+* D)
491	    (hincl : incl.comp (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
492	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))
493	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) :
494	    (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
495	      = (((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
496	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D)).submatrix id
497	          (fun j : Fin d => (X.orderIsoOfFin hX j : Fin r)))⁻¹ *
498	        (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
499	          (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ) D) := by
500	  have hmm : (imageMatrix d r I X hI hX).map incl
501	      = (universalMinorInv d r I X hI hX).map incl
502	        * ((universalMatrix d r I hI).map
503	            (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
504	              (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I X hI hX)))).map incl := by
505	    rw [imageMatrix]; exact Matrix.map_mul
506	  rw [hmm, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hincl, universalMinorInv,
507	    ← map_nonsing_inv incl (universalMinor d r I X hI hX)
508	        (isUnit_det_universalMinor d r I X hI hX)]
509	  congr 1
510	  rw [universalMinor, map_map_eq_of_comp _ _ _ _ hincl, ← Matrix.submatrix_map]
511	
512	/-- Matrix cancellation `(B⁻¹ A)(A⁻¹ M) = B⁻¹ M` when `A` is invertible. Project-local helper
513	for the final step of the cocycle. -/
514	private lemma inv_mul_inv_mul_cancel {d e : ℕ} {R : Type*} [CommRing R]
515	    (A B : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin d) R) (M : Matrix (Fin d) (Fin e) R) (hA : IsUnit A.det) :
516	    (B⁻¹ * A) * (A⁻¹ * M) = B⁻¹ * M := by
517	  rw [Matrix.mul_assoc B⁻¹ A (A⁻¹ * M), ← Matrix.mul_assoc A A⁻¹ M,
518	    Matrix.mul_nonsing_inv A hA, Matrix.one_mul]
519	
520	/-- The central matrix identity behind the cocycle condition: over the triple-overlap ring
521	`S_I`, the image matrix `(X^I_K)⁻¹ X^I` of `θ_{I,K}` equals `θ_{I,J}` applied entrywise to the
522	image matrix `(X^J_K)⁻¹ X^J` of `θ_{J,K}`. Both reduce to `(Y_K)⁻¹ Y` with `Y = X^I` over
523	`S_I`. Project-local. -/
524	private lemma cocycle_imageMatrix_eq (d r : ℕ) (I J K : Finset (Fin r)) (hI : I.card = d)
525	    (hJ : J.card = d) (hK : K.card = d) :
526	    (imageMatrix d r I K hI hK).map
527	        (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
528	      = (imageMatrix d r J K hJ hK).map
529	          ((cocycleΘIJ d r I J K hI hJ hK).comp
530	            (awayInclRight (minorDet d r J I hJ hI) (minorDet d r J K hJ hK))) := by
531	  -- LHS = (Y_K)⁻¹ * Y, where `Y := X^I` over `S_I`.
532	  have hLHS := imageMatrix_map_eq d r I K hI hK
533	    (awayInclRight (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
534	    (awayInclRight_comp_algebraMap _ _)
535	  -- `(imageMatrix I J).map (awayInclLeft …) = (Y_J)⁻¹ Y`.
536	  have hMJimg := imageMatrix_map_eq d r I J hI hJ
537	    (awayInclLeft (minorDet d r I J hI hJ) (minorDet d r I K hI hK))
538	    (awayInclLeft_comp_algebraMap _ _)
539	  set Y := (universalMatrix d r I hI).map
540	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)
541	        (Localization.Away (minorDet d r I J hI hJ * minorDet d r I K hI hK))) with hY
542	  -- Unit facts for the two minors of `Y`.
543	  have hYJ : IsUnit (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det := by
544	    have e : (Y.submatrix id (fun j : Fin d => (J.orderIsoOfFin hJ j : Fin r))).det
545	        = algebraMap (MvPolynomial (Fin d × {q : Fin r // q ∉ I}) ℤ)

9	/-!
10	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
11	
12	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
13	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
14	base change on the target.
15	
16	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
17	```
18	  X' --g'--> X
19	  |f'        |f
20	  v          v
21	  S' --g---> S
22	```
23	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
24	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
25	
26	The three main declarations are:
27	
28	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
29	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
30	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
32	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
33	  associativity).
34	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
35	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
36	
37	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
38	
39	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
40	Tag 02KH.
41	-/
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	open Scheme.Modules
50	
51	open scoped TensorProduct
52	
53	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
54	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
55	
56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
67	
68	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
69	```
70	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
71	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
72	```
73	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
75	commutativity of the square.
76	
77	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
78	base-change diagram. -/
79	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
81	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
82	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
83	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
84	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
85	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
86	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)
87	
88	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — locality of isomorphisms for `Scheme.Modules`
89	
90	Mathlib provides the per-open criterion `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`
91	(`IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U, IsIso (φ.app U)`) and the stalkwise criterion
92	`TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso` for `TopCat.Sheaf`-valued morphisms,
93	but it does not package the stalk-local criterion at the level of `Scheme.Modules`
94	morphisms. The following lemmas bridge that gap; they are the locality tools needed for
95	the affine reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (where one checks the
96	base-change map after restricting to / taking stalks over affine opens). -/
97	
98	/-- **Stalk-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** A morphism `φ : M ⟶ N`
99	of sheaves of modules over a scheme `X` is an isomorphism iff its underlying morphism of
100	abelian presheaves is a stalkwise isomorphism. Project-local: Mathlib only ships the
101	per-open criterion and the `TopCat.Sheaf`-level stalk criterion separately. -/
102	theorem Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
103	    (φ : M ⟶ N) :
104	    IsIso φ ↔ ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map
105	      ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ)) := by
106	  constructor
107	  · intro h x
108	    haveI : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) := Functor.map_isIso _ _
109	    exact Functor.map_isIso _ _
110	  · intro h
111	    -- Package the underlying abelian presheaves as `TopCat.Sheaf`es.
112	    let MS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨M.presheaf, M.isSheaf⟩
113	    let NS : TopCat.Sheaf Ab.{u} X := ⟨N.presheaf, N.isSheaf⟩
114	    let fS : MS ⟶ NS := ⟨(Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ⟩
115	    haveI : ∀ x : X, IsIso ((TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor Ab.{u} x).map fS.hom) := h
116	    haveI hSiso : IsIso fS := TopCat.Presheaf.isIso_of_stalkFunctor_map_iso fS
117	    have h1 : IsIso ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ) :=
118	      (TopCat.Sheaf.forget Ab.{u} X).map_isIso fS
119	    exact (CategoryTheory.isIso_iff_of_reflects_iso φ (Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X)).mp h1
120	
121	/-- **Basis-local criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** If `B` is a basis of
122	opens of `X` and `φ : M ⟶ N` restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every basic
123	open `B i`, then `φ` is an isomorphism. This reduces iso-checking from *all* opens (the
124	content of `Scheme.Modules.Hom.isIso_iff_isIso_app`) to a chosen basis. Project-local:
125	Mathlib provides the stalkwise pieces (`germ_exist_of_isBasis`,
126	`stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis`) but not the packaged criterion at the
127	`Scheme.Modules` level. -/
128	theorem Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
129	    {ι : Type*} {B : ι → X.Opens} (hB : TopologicalSpace.Opens.IsBasis (Set.range B))
130	    (φ : M ⟶ N) (h : ∀ i, IsIso (φ.app (B i))) : IsIso φ := by
131	  -- Reduce to a stalkwise isomorphism of the underlying `Ab`-presheaf morphism `α`.
132	  rw [Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_stalkFunctor_map]
133	  intro x
134	  -- `α.app (op (B i))` is definitionally `φ.app (B i)`, hence an isomorphism on each basic open.
135	  have happ : ∀ U ∈ Set.range B,
136	      IsIso (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U)) := by
137	    rintro U ⟨i, rfl⟩; exact h i
138	  rw [CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
139	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
140	  · -- Injectivity of the stalk map from injectivity on a basis.
141	    refine TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_injective_of_isBasis hB ?_ x
142	    intro U hU
143	    haveI := happ U hU
144	    exact (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso
145	      (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U))).injective
146	  · -- Surjectivity: a germ at `x` comes from a section over a basic open, where `α` is onto.
147	    intro t
148	    obtain ⟨U, hxU, hU, s, rfl⟩ :=
149	      TopCat.Presheaf.germ_exist_of_isBasis hB N.presheaf x t
150	    haveI := happ U hU
151	    obtain ⟨s', hs'⟩ := (CategoryTheory.ConcreteCategory.bijective_of_isIso
152	      (((Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf X).map φ).app (Opposite.op U))).surjective s
153	    refine ⟨M.presheaf.germ U x hxU s', ?_⟩
154	    erw [TopCat.Presheaf.stalkFunctor_map_germ_apply]
155	    rw [hs']
156	    rfl

158	/-- **Affine-open locality criterion for isomorphisms of `𝒪_X`-modules.** A morphism
159	`φ : M ⟶ N` of sheaves of modules is an isomorphism iff it restricts to an isomorphism on
160	the sections over every affine open of `X`. This is the precise reduction used in the
161	affine proof of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`: the affine opens of `X` form a basis,
162	and over an affine open the base-change map becomes the pure ring-theoretic
163	`cancelBaseChange` isomorphism. -/
164	theorem Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens {X : Scheme.{u}} {M N : X.Modules}
165	    (φ : M ⟶ N) : IsIso φ ↔ ∀ U : X.affineOpens, IsIso (φ.app U) := by
166	  refine ⟨fun _ U => inferInstance, fun h => ?_⟩
167	  exact Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
168	    (B := (Subtype.val : X.affineOpens → X.Opens))
169	    (by simpa [Subtype.range_val] using X.isBasis_affineOpens) φ h

245	slice/over-category restriction shown QC, or a direct `Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis`
246	construction of the object iso on basic opens via `IsLocalizedModule`
247	(`(restrictScalars φ M)` localised at `a` = `M` localised at `φ a`). -/
248	
249	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — global-sections / pushforward ring map -/
250	
251	/-- The global-sections comparison ring map underlying `Spec.map φ` at the top open is
252	conjugate to `φ` via the global-sections isomorphisms `Γ(Spec -) ≅ -`. Concretely, the
253	square
254	```
255	  R  --gsR.hom-->  Γ(Spec R, ⊤)
256	  |φ                     |(Spec.map φ).appTop
257	  v                      v
258	  R' --gsR'.hom--> Γ(Spec R', ⊤)
259	```
260	commutes, where `gsR = StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso R`. This is the ring-level
261	heart of the affine pushforward-of-tilde identification (`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`):
262	it is exactly the `eqToIso` hypothesis required by the element-free
263	`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'` route for the Γ-fragment iso (route (b) above), since
264	`moduleSpecΓFunctor` builds its `R`-action by `restrictScalars` along `gsR.hom` and the
265	pushforward builds its `Γ(Spec R, ⊤)`-action by `restrictScalars` along
266	`(Spec.map φ).appTop`. Project-local: Mathlib ships the underlying
267	`Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality` but not this `globalSectionsIso`/`appTop` form. -/
268	theorem globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
269	    (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom ≫ (Spec.map φ).appTop
270	      = φ ≫ (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom := by
271	  have hR : (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R).inv := rfl
272	  have hR' : (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom = (Scheme.ΓSpecIso R').inv := rfl
273	  rw [hR, hR']
274	  exact (Scheme.ΓSpecIso_inv_naturality φ).symm
275	
276	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Γ of an affine pushforward -/
277	
278	/-- **Global sections of an affine pushforward = restriction of scalars.** For a ring
279	map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and *any* sheaf of modules `N` on `Spec R'`, the `R`-module of global
280	sections of the pushforward `(Spec φ)_* N` is the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the
281	`R'`-module of global sections of `N`. Built element-free (route (b)): both sides peel
282	(by `rfl`) to nested `ModuleCat.restrictScalars` towers over the common global-section
283	module `Γ(N, ⊤)`; the two towers are reconciled by collapsing each via
284	`ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App` and applying the ring equation
285	`globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop`. No element-level scalar multiplication is
286	touched, dodging the carrier-instance wall. Project-local: the affine companion of the
287	global-sections fragment of flat base change. -/
288	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
289	    (N : (Spec R').Modules) :
290	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj N) ≅
291	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj ((moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R')).obj N) := by
292	  set SecN : ModuleCat ↑((Spec R').ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
293	    (((SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R').ringCatSheaf (Opposite.op ⊤)
294	        (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop)).obj N).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) with hSecN
295	  set gsRhom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom.hom with hgsR
296	  set gsR'hom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom.hom with hgsR'
297	  set pushTop := ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom with hpush
298	  have hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp φ.hom := by
299	    apply RingHom.ext
300	    intro x
301	    simpa [hpush, hgsR, hgsR', RingHom.comp_apply] using
302	      congr($(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop φ).hom x)
303	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App gsRhom pushTop (pushTop.comp gsRhom) rfl SecN).symm ≪≫
304	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app SecN ≪≫
305	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom gsR'hom (gsR'hom.comp φ.hom) rfl SecN)
306	
307	/-- **Γ-fragment for a tilde-module.** Specialising `gammaPushforwardIso` to `N = M^~`
308	and using the unit iso `tilde.toTildeΓNatIso` (`Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≅ M`), the `R`-module of global
309	sections of `(Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This is the
310	`Γ`-fragment comparison used to recognise the section-level base-change map in the affine
311	reduction of `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`. Project-local: corollary of
312	`gammaPushforwardIso`. -/
313	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardTildeIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
314	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
315	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).obj
316	        ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M)) ≅
317	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M :=
318	  gammaPushforwardIso φ (tilde M) ≪≫
319	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).mapIso (tilde.toTildeΓNatIso.app M).symm
320	
321	/-- **Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open = restriction of scalars.**
322	The `D(a)`-level (indeed arbitrary-open) generalization of `gammaPushforwardIso`. For a ring map
323	`φ : R ⟶ R'`, an `Spec R'`-module `N`, and an open `U` of `Spec R` with preimage
324	`V = (Spec φ)⁻¹ U` in `Spec R'`, the `R`-module of sections of the pushforward
325	`(Spec φ)_* N` over `U` is the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-module of sections of
326	`N` over `V`. Because `modulesSpecToSheaf` forgets to the *global* section ring uniformly (it
327	restricts scalars along the global-sections map at the top open, not at `U`), the construction is
328	*identical* to that of `gammaPushforwardIso` — the same restriction-of-scalars composition identity
329	twice plus the same `⊤`-level ring equation `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop` — with only
330	the evaluation open changed from `⊤` to `U` / `V`. Project-local. -/
331	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardIsoAt {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
332	    (N : (Spec R').Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens) :
333	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj N)).val.obj
334	        (Opposite.op U) ≅
335	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj
336	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj
337	          (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) := by
338	  set SecN : ModuleCat ↑((Spec R').ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
339	    (((SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R').ringCatSheaf (Opposite.op ⊤)
340	        (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop)).obj N).val.obj
341	          (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) with hSecN
342	  set gsRhom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom.hom with hgsR
343	  set gsR'hom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom.hom with hgsR'
344	  set pushTop := ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom with hpush
345	  have hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp φ.hom := by
346	    apply RingHom.ext
347	    intro x
348	    simpa [hpush, hgsR, hgsR', RingHom.comp_apply] using
349	      congr($(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop φ).hom x)
350	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App gsRhom pushTop (pushTop.comp gsRhom) rfl SecN).symm ≪≫
351	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app SecN ≪≫
352	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom gsR'hom (gsR'hom.comp φ.hom) rfl SecN)
353	
354	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — restriction of scalars and localization -/
355	
356	/-- **Counit of the tilde–Γ adjunction is a basic-open isomorphism whenever the restriction
357	of the global sections is a localization.** For a sheaf of modules `N` on `Spec R` and `a : R`,
358	if the structure-sheaf restriction map `Γ(N, ⊤) → Γ(N, D(a))` (read in `ModuleCat R` via the
359	global-sections forgetful functor `modulesSpecToSheaf`) exhibits `Γ(N, D(a))` as the localization
360	of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `Submonoid.powers a`, then the counit `fromTildeΓ N` is an isomorphism on the
361	sections over `D(a)`. This is the section-level engine of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it isolates
362	the *only* nontrivial input (`Γ(N, D(a)) = Γ(N, ⊤)[1/a]`) and discharges the rest — the comparison
363	`fromTildeΓ` is, on `D(a)`, the canonical map between two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` (the tilde
364	localization `toOpen` and the restriction), hence an isomorphism by the uniqueness of localized
365	modules. Project-local: Mathlib has the localization-uniqueness lemmas but not this packaging at
366	the level of `fromTildeΓ`. -/
367	lemma fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : (Spec R).Modules)
368	    (a : R)
369	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
370	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map
371	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom] :
372	    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)) := by
373	  -- The underlying function of `Hom.app` agrees (by `rfl`) with that of the `modulesSpecToSheaf`
374	  -- image, where the tilde–Γ triangle identity `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` applies.
375	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
376	  have hfun : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
377	        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) =
378	      ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
379	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) :=
380	    rfl
381	  rw [hfun]
382	  -- Abbreviations: `j` is the tilde localization map, `ρ` the structure-sheaf restriction, both
383	  -- localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `powers a`; `L` is the section map of the counit.
384	  set j := (tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
385	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom with hj
386	  set ρ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map
387	    (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom with hρ
388	  haveI hρinst : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ρ := by rw [hρ]; infer_instance
389	  clear_value ρ
390	  set L := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
391	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))).hom with hL
392	  -- The triangle identity gives `L ∘ₗ j = ρ`.
393	  have htri : L ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
394	    have := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
395	    apply_fun ModuleCat.Hom.hom at this
396	    simpa [hL, hj, hρ, ModuleCat.hom_comp] using this
397	  -- `L` equals the canonical iso between the two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)`, hence bijective.
398	  set ej := IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers a) j with hej
399	  set eρ := IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers a) ρ with heρ
400	  let e := ej.symm.trans eρ
401	  have hcomp : e.toLinearMap ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
402	    ext x
403	    simp only [e, hej, heρ, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
404	      LinearEquiv.trans_apply, IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_apply]
405	    exact IsLocalizedModule.iso_mk_one (Submonoid.powers a) ρ x
406	  have hLeq : L = e.toLinearMap :=
407	    IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) j (IsLocalizedModule.map_units ρ)
408	      (htri.trans hcomp.symm)
409	  rw [show ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
410	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) = ⇑L from rfl, hLeq]
411	  exact e.bijective
412	
413	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional on the basic-open localization fact.**
414	This is the full assembly of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (route iii), modulo the single
415	remaining ingredient: that on each basic open `D(a)` the structure-sheaf restriction of the
416	pushforward `(Spec φ)_* M^~` exhibits its sections over `D(a)` as the localization at
417	`Submonoid.powers a` of its global sections. Given that hypothesis (`hloc`), the counit
418	`fromTildeΓ ((Spec φ)_* M^~)` is an isomorphism (basis-locality criterion
419	`Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` over the basic opens, with each section iso supplied by
420	`fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule`), so `(Spec φ)_* M^~` lies in the essential image of
421	`tilde` and the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` upgrades to the desired
422	object isomorphism.
423	
424	The hypothesis `hloc` is the *only* outstanding obligation for the unconditional
425	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it is the affine "`(Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a)`, and localizing
426	`restrict φ M` at `a` agrees with localizing `M` at `φ a`" fact (Stacks, widetilde-pullback),
427	whose ring-change core is `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` below. Discharging it
428	requires identifying the `modulesSpecToSheaf` global-ring `R`-action on the sections of the
429	pushforward over `D(a)` with restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-localization
430	`M[1/φ a]` — the section-level structure-sheaf naturality at `D(a)`. -/
431	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
432	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R')
433	    (hloc : ∀ a : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
434	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))).val.map
435	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom) :
436	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
437	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
438	  have hiso : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
439	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))) := by
440	    apply Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
441	      (B := fun a : R => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a) PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
442	    intro a
443	    haveI := hloc a
444	    exact fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule _ a
445	  exact (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ _)).symm ≪≫
446	    (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)
447	
448	/-- **Restriction of scalars of a localized module is a localized module.** If `f : M →ₗ[A] N`
449	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at the image submonoid `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S`
450	(for `S : Submonoid R` and `A` an `R`-algebra), then the `R`-linear map underlying `f`
451	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `S` itself. This is the exact converse of
452	`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` and is the ring-change ingredient powering the affine
453	pushforward identification: localizing `restrictScalars φ M` at `a ∈ R` agrees with localizing
454	`M` at `φ a ∈ R'`. Project-local: Mathlib ships only the forward direction. -/
455	lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
456	    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
457	    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
458	    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
459	    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
460	    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
461	    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
462	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
463	  map_units x := by
464	    have h := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
465	      (⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)
466	    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at h ⊢
467	    exact h
468	  surj y := by
469	    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
470	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
471	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
472	    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
473	      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
474	    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩
475	
476	/-- **The structure-sheaf restriction of a tilde-module from `⊤` to `D(b)` is a localization.**
477	Read in `ModuleCat R'` via `modulesSpecToSheaf`, the restriction map
478	`Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(b))` exhibits its target as the localization of its source at
479	`Submonoid.powers b`. This packages the Mathlib instance
480	`tilde.toOpen … (basicOpen b)` (which makes `M → Γ(M^~, D(b))` a localization) together with the
481	triangle identity `tilde.toOpen_res` and the fact that `M → Γ(M^~, ⊤)` is bijective (localization
482	at the trivial submonoid `powers 1`). Project-local. -/
483	lemma tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule {R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') (b : R') :
484	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b)
485	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
486	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
487	  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is a localization at `powers (1 : R')` (since `basicOpen 1 = ⊤`), hence bijective.
488	  haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
489	    have h := (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R'))
490	      (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R'))).hom)
491	    rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h
492	    exact h
493	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
494	    refine ⟨fun x y hxy => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
495	    · obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
496	        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
497	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
498	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
499	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul, one_smul] at hc
500	      exact hc
501	    · obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
502	        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
503	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
504	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
505	      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
506	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul] at hc
507	      exact hc.symm
508	  -- Triangle identity: `toOpen ⊤ ≫ (restriction) = toOpen (D b)`.
509	  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
510	    (homOfLE (le_top))
511	  -- The inverse equivalence `Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≃ M`.
512	  set le : M ≃ₗ[R'] ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
513	    LinearEquiv.ofBijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom hbij with hle
514	  -- The triangle at the level of linear maps: `restriction ∘ₗ le = toOpen (D b)`.
515	  have htri2 : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
516	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom ∘ₗ le.toLinearMap
517	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom := by
518	    have h := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom htri
519	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at h
520	    exact h
521	  -- Hence `restriction = toOpen (D b) ∘ₗ le⁻¹`, a localization at `powers b` precomposed with
522	  -- a linear equivalence.
523	  have key : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
524	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom
525	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom ∘ₗ le.symm.toLinearMap :=
526	    (LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm _ _).mpr htri2
527	  rw [key]
528	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers b)
529	    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom le.symm
530	
531	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module (unconditional).** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'`
532	and an `R'`-module `M`, pushing the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` forward along `Spec φ` is,
533	up to canonical isomorphism, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This
534	is the unconditional form of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`: the latter's
535	hypothesis `hloc` (each structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` is a localization at
536	`powers a`) is discharged here by the `D(a)`-level transport. See blueprint
537	`lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
538	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
539	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
540	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
541	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
542	  apply pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule φ M
543	  intro a
544	  -- STRATEGY (`of_linearEquiv`): the restriction map `ρ : Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` of
545	  -- `N := (Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is, under the isomorphisms
546	  -- `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`,
547	  -- identified with the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-side tilde restriction
548	  -- `σ : Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))`. That `σ` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`
549	  -- (`tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`), so its restriction of scalars along `φ` is a
550	  -- localization at `powers a` (`IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`); transporting along
551	  -- the two isomorphisms then yields `hloc(a)`.
552	  -- The two bricks for this discharge are now in place and axiom-clean:

657	/-- **Naturality of the Γ-fragment comparison.** The per-object isomorphisms
658	`gammaPushforwardIso φ N : Γ_R((Spec φ)_* N) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'} N)` assemble into a
659	natural isomorphism of functors `(Spec R').Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R`
660	\[ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \;\circ\; \Gamma_R
661	   \;\cong\; \Gamma_{R'} \;\circ\; \operatorname{restr}_\varphi . \]
662	Every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIso` is the identity on underlying elements (the
663	`restrictScalarsComp'App` isos and `restrictScalarsCongr` merely repackage the module structure
664	on the unchanged carrier), so naturality is a pointwise `rfl`. This is the right-adjoint natural
665	isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
666	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
668	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
669	      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
670	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
671	    intro N N' g
672	    ext x
673	    rfl)
674	
675	/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
676	pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
677	tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
678	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).
679	
680	The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
681	to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
682	`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
683	left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
684	`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
685	`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
686	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
687	at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
688	blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
689	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
690	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
691	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
692	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
693	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
694	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
695	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
696	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M

699	
700	/-- **The pullback cone legs are the `Spec`-maps of the tensor inclusions.** For ring maps
701	`ψ : R ⟶ R'` and `φ : R ⟶ A` (regarding `A`, `R'` as `R`-algebras via `φ`, `ψ`), over the generic
702	pullback square `Limits.pullback (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)` Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso` identifies
703	the total space with `Spec (A ⊗[R] R')`, and under it the two cone legs are the `Spec`-maps of the
704	canonical tensor inclusions `Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom : A → A ⊗[R] R'` and
705	`Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight : R' → A ⊗[R] R'`. The only content beyond Mathlib's
706	`pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst`/`_inv_snd` is the structure bridging `Spec.map φ ↔ Spec.map (algebraMap
707	R A)` (definitional once `A` carries the `φ`-algebra structure). See blueprint
708	`lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor`. -/
709	theorem pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}} (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) :
710	    letI : Algebra R A := φ.hom.toAlgebra
711	    letI : Algebra R R' := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
712	    ((pullbackSpecIso (R := ↑R) (S := ↑A) (T := ↑R')).inv ≫
713	        Limits.pullback.fst (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
714	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom Algebra.TensorProduct.includeLeftRingHom)) ∧
715	      ((pullbackSpecIso (R := ↑R) (S := ↑A) (T := ↑R')).inv ≫
716	        Limits.pullback.snd (Spec.map φ) (Spec.map ψ)
717	        = Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
718	          (Algebra.TensorProduct.includeRight (R := ↑R) (A := ↑A) (B := ↑R')).toRingHom)) := by
719	  letI : Algebra R A := φ.hom.toAlgebra
720	  letI : Algebra R R' := ψ.hom.toAlgebra
721	  refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
722	  · exact pullbackSpecIso_inv_fst ↑R ↑A ↑R'
723	  · exact pullbackSpecIso_inv_snd ↑R ↑A ↑R'

735	dictionaries apply directly with no pullback-leg identification needed. See blueprint
736	`lem:base_change_mate_domain_read`. -/
737	noncomputable def base_change_mate_domain_read {R R' A : CommRingCat.{u}}
738	    (ψ : R ⟶ R') (φ : R ⟶ A) (M : ModuleCat.{u} A) :

8	/-!
9	# Graded Hilbert–Serre rationality layer

11	This file houses the `IsRatHilb` toolkit and the `GradedModule` ambient
12	subquotient induction (`subquotient_hilbertSeries_rational`) that establish
13	the rationality of graded Hilbert series (Stacks 00K1). The keystone
14	`gradedModule_hilbertSeries_rational` shows that for a graded `κ`-module
15	finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` with finite-dimensional components,

19	graded-algebra layer and the Quot/Grassmannian-defs layer can be proved in

32	lemma coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul (F : ℚ⟦X⟧) (n : ℕ) :
33	    ((PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val * F).coeff n
34	      = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (n + 1), F.coeff k := by
35	  have h1 : (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val = PowerSeries.mk (fun _ => (1 : ℚ)) := by

37	    simpa using this
38	  rw [h1, PowerSeries.coeff_mul, Finset.Nat.sum_antidiagonal_eq_sum_range_succ_mk]
39	  simp only [PowerSeries.coeff_mk, one_mul]
40	  rw [← Finset.sum_range_reflect (fun k => F.coeff k) (n + 1)]
41	  apply Finset.sum_congr rfl
42	  intro x _
43	  congr 1
