45	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
46	/-- **Antidifference step for rational Hilbert series.** If the first difference
47	`H (n+1) - H n` is, for `n ≫ 0`, the `n`-th coefficient of the rational series
48	`q · (1-X)^{-e}`, then `H` itself is, for `n ≫ 0`, the `n`-th coefficient of
49	`p · (1-X)^{-(e+1)}` for an explicit polynomial `p`. This is the power-series
50	heart of the inductive step in graded Hilbert–Serre (Stacks 00K1). Project-local:
51	Mathlib supplies only the converse extraction `Polynomial.existsUnique_hilbertPoly`. -/
52	lemma rationalHilbert_antidiff
53	    (H δ : ℕ → ℚ) (q : Polynomial ℚ) (e N : ℕ)
54	    (hδ : ∀ n, N < n → δ n = ((q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ e).val).coeff n)
55	    (hH : ∀ n, N < n → H (n + 1) - H n = δ (n + 1)) :
56	    ∃ (p : Polynomial ℚ), ∀ n, N < n →
57	      H n = ((p : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val).coeff n := by
58	  set F : ℚ⟦X⟧ := (q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ e).val with hF
59	  -- Partial-sum identity: the order-`(e+1)` series accumulates the order-`e` coefficients.
60	  have hsum : ∀ m, ((q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val).coeff m
61	      = ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (m + 1), F.coeff k := by
62	    intro m
63	    have hmul : (q : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val
64	        = (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val * F := by
65	      rw [hF, show (e + 1) = 1 + e from by omega, PowerSeries.invOneSubPow_add, Units.val_mul]
66	      ring
67	    rw [hmul, coeff_invOneSubPow_one_mul]
68	  -- Telescoping `H` from its first differences, expressed via `F`.
69	  have hstep : ∀ n, N < n → H (n + 1) - H n = F.coeff (n + 1) := by
70	    intro n hn
71	    rw [hH n hn, hδ (n + 1) (by omega)]
72	  have htel : ∀ j, H (N + 1 + j)
73	      = H (N + 1) + ∑ i ∈ Finset.range j, F.coeff (N + 2 + i) := by
74	    intro j
75	    induction j with
76	    | zero => simp
77	    | succ j ih =>
78	        rw [Finset.sum_range_succ, show N + 2 + j = N + 1 + (j + 1) from by omega]
79	        have hs := hstep (N + 1 + j) (by omega)
80	        rw [show (N + 1 + j) + 1 = N + 1 + (j + 1) from by omega] at hs
81	        linarith [hs, ih]
82	  -- Constant-absorption: a constant function is the order-`(e+1)` coefficient of `C·(1-X)^e`.
83	  have hCconst : ∀ (c : ℚ),
84	      c • (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val
85	        = ((Polynomial.C c * (1 - Polynomial.X) ^ e : Polynomial ℚ) : ℚ⟦X⟧)
86	            * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val := by
87	    intro c
88	    have hkey : (1 - PowerSeries.X : ℚ⟦X⟧) ^ e * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (e + 1)).val
89	        = (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val := by
90	      rw [Nat.add_comm e 1]
91	      exact PowerSeries.one_sub_pow_mul_invOneSubPow_val_add_eq_invOneSubPow_val ℚ 1 e
92	    rw [Polynomial.coe_mul, Polynomial.coe_C, Polynomial.coe_pow, Polynomial.coe_sub,
93	      Polynomial.coe_one, Polynomial.coe_X, mul_assoc, hkey, PowerSeries.smul_eq_C_mul]
94	  have hcoeff1 : ∀ m, (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val.coeff m = 1 := by
95	    intro m
96	    have h1 : (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val = PowerSeries.mk (fun _ => (1 : ℚ)) := by
97	      have := PowerSeries.invOneSubPow_val_succ_eq_mk_add_choose (S := ℚ) (d := 0)
98	      simpa using this
99	    rw [h1, PowerSeries.coeff_mk]
100	  -- Assemble the polynomial numerator.
101	  set B : ℚ := ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (N + 2), F.coeff k with hB
102	  set C0 : ℚ := H (N + 1) - B with hC0
103	  refine ⟨Polynomial.C C0 * (1 - Polynomial.X) ^ e + q, ?_⟩
104	  intro n hn
105	  -- Rewrite `H n` via the telescoping identity at `j = n - (N+1)`.
106	  obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ : ∃ j, n = N + 1 + j := ⟨n - (N + 1), by omega⟩
107	  rw [htel j]
108	  -- The tail sum is an `Ico`-window of `F`.
109	  have htail : ∑ i ∈ Finset.range j, F.coeff (N + 2 + i)
110	      = ∑ k ∈ Finset.Ico (N + 2) (N + 2 + j), F.coeff k := by
111	    rw [Finset.sum_Ico_eq_sum_range]
112	    simp
113	  rw [htail]
114	  -- Split `range (n+1) = range (N+2) ∪ Ico (N+2) (n+1)` in the partial-sum identity.
115	  have hsplit : ∑ k ∈ Finset.range (N + 1 + j + 1), F.coeff k
116	      = B + ∑ k ∈ Finset.Ico (N + 2) (N + 2 + j), F.coeff k := by
117	    rw [hB, Finset.range_eq_Ico, Finset.range_eq_Ico,
118	      show N + 1 + j + 1 = N + 2 + j from by omega,
119	      ← Finset.sum_Ico_consecutive _ (Nat.zero_le (N + 2)) (by omega : N + 2 ≤ N + 2 + j)]
120	  -- Now compute the target coefficient and match.
121	  rw [show ((Polynomial.C C0 * (1 - Polynomial.X) ^ e + q : Polynomial ℚ) : ℚ⟦X⟧)
122	        = ((Polynomial.C C0 * (1 - Polynomial.X) ^ e : Polynomial ℚ) : ℚ⟦X⟧) + (q : ℚ⟦X⟧)
123	      from by push_cast; ring,
124	    add_mul, map_add, ← hCconst C0]
125	  rw [show (C0 • (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val).coeff (N + 1 + j)
126	        = C0 * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ 1).val.coeff (N + 1 + j)
127	      from by rw [map_smul]; rfl, hcoeff1, mul_one,
128	    hsum (N + 1 + j), hsplit, hC0]
129	  ring
130	
131	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
132	/-- Internal predicate for graded Hilbert–Serre: `f : ℕ → ℚ` is, for `n ≫ 0`, the
133	`n`-th coefficient of the rational power series `p · (1-X)^{-d}` for some numerator
134	polynomial `p`. The closure lemmas below (`bump`, `sub`, `shiftRight`, `antidiff`,
135	`ofEventuallyZero`) are the inductive toolkit for the rationality proof. -/
136	def IsRatHilb (f : ℕ → ℚ) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
137	  ∃ (p : Polynomial ℚ) (N : ℕ), ∀ n, N < n →
138	    f n = ((p : ℚ⟦X⟧) * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ d).val).coeff n
139	
140	/-- An eventually-zero Hilbert function is rational of order `0` (numerator `0`). -/
141	lemma IsRatHilb.ofEventuallyZero {f : ℕ → ℚ} (N : ℕ) (hf : ∀ n, N < n → f n = 0) :
142	    IsRatHilb f 0 := by
143	  refine ⟨0, N, fun n hn => ?_⟩
144	  rw [hf n hn]
145	  simp
146	
147	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
148	/-- The order of a rational Hilbert function may be raised by one (multiply the
149	numerator by `(1-X)`); this lets two series be brought to a common denominator. -/
150	lemma IsRatHilb.bump {f : ℕ → ℚ} {d : ℕ} (h : IsRatHilb f d) :
151	    IsRatHilb f (d + 1) := by
152	  obtain ⟨p, N, hp⟩ := h
153	  refine ⟨p * (1 - Polynomial.X), N, fun n hn => ?_⟩
154	  rw [hp n hn]
155	  congr 1
156	  have hkey : (1 - PowerSeries.X : ℚ⟦X⟧) ^ 1 * (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ (d + 1)).val
157	      = (PowerSeries.invOneSubPow ℚ d).val :=
158	    PowerSeries.one_sub_pow_mul_invOneSubPow_val_add_eq_invOneSubPow_val ℚ d 1
159	  rw [pow_one] at hkey
160	  rw [Polynomial.coe_mul, Polynomial.coe_sub, Polynomial.coe_one, Polynomial.coe_X,
161	    mul_assoc, hkey]
162	
163	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
164	/-- Rational Hilbert functions of the same order are closed under pointwise difference. -/
165	lemma IsRatHilb.sub {f g : ℕ → ℚ} {d : ℕ} (hf : IsRatHilb f d) (hg : IsRatHilb g d) :
166	    IsRatHilb (fun n => f n - g n) d := by
167	  obtain ⟨p, Np, hp⟩ := hf
168	  obtain ⟨q, Nq, hq⟩ := hg
169	  refine ⟨p - q, max Np Nq, fun n hn => ?_⟩
170	  simp only
171	  rw [hp n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hn), hq n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hn),
172	    Polynomial.coe_sub, sub_mul, map_sub]
173	
174	open PowerSeries Polynomial in
175	/-- Right-shift closure: if `f` is rational of order `d`, so is `n ↦ f (n-1)`
176	(multiply the numerator by `X`). -/
177	lemma IsRatHilb.shiftRight {f : ℕ → ℚ} {d : ℕ} (h : IsRatHilb f d) :
178	    IsRatHilb (fun n => f (n - 1)) d := by
179	  obtain ⟨p, N, hp⟩ := h
180	  refine ⟨Polynomial.X * p, N + 1, fun n hn => ?_⟩
181	  obtain ⟨m, rfl⟩ : ∃ m, n = m + 1 := ⟨n - 1, by omega⟩
182	  simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel]
183	  rw [hp m (by omega), Polynomial.coe_mul, Polynomial.coe_X, mul_assoc,
184	    PowerSeries.coeff_succ_X_mul]
185	
186	/-- The antidifference step, packaged for the predicate: if `g` is rational of order
187	`e` and `H (n+1) - H n = g (n+1)` eventually, then `H` is rational of order `e+1`. -/
188	lemma IsRatHilb.antidiff {H g : ℕ → ℚ} {e N : ℕ} (hg : IsRatHilb g e)
189	    (hH : ∀ n, N < n → H (n + 1) - H n = g (n + 1)) : IsRatHilb H (e + 1) := by
190	  obtain ⟨q, Ng, hq⟩ := hg
191	  obtain ⟨p, hp⟩ := rationalHilbert_antidiff H g q e (max N Ng)
192	    (fun n hn => hq n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_right _ _) hn))
193	    (fun n hn => hH n (lt_of_le_of_lt (le_max_left _ _) hn))
194	  exact ⟨p, max N Ng, hp⟩
195	
196	/-- **Inductive-step engine for graded Hilbert–Serre (Stacks 00K1).** The entire
197	power-series side of the inductive step: if the Hilbert function `hM` of `M` has
198	first difference matching the alternating sum `hC (n+1) - hK n` of the Hilbert
199	functions of the cokernel `C = M/xM` and kernel `K = ker(x : M → M(1))` — the
200	content of the degreewise short exact sequence `0 → K_n → M_n → M_{n+1} → C_{n+1} → 0`
201	— and both `hC, hK` are rational of order `d`, then `hM` is rational of order `d+1`.
202	The only remaining (graded-algebra) obligation in the rationality proof is to produce
203	`hK, hC` with this difference identity and apply the induction hypothesis. -/
204	lemma IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq {hM hC hK : ℕ → ℚ} {d N : ℕ}
205	    (hC' : IsRatHilb hC d) (hK' : IsRatHilb hK d)
206	    (hdiff : ∀ n, N < n → hM (n + 1) - hM n = hC (n + 1) - hK n) :
207	    IsRatHilb hM (d + 1) := by
208	  have hg : IsRatHilb (fun n => hC n - hK (n - 1)) d := hC'.sub hK'.shiftRight
209	  refine IsRatHilb.antidiff (g := fun n => hC n - hK (n - 1)) (N := N) hg ?_
210	  intro n hn
211	  simp only [Nat.add_sub_cancel]
212	  exact hdiff n hn
213	
214	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — graded-module API for Stacks 00K1
215	
216	This namespace builds the graded-module side of the Stacks 00K1 inductive step
217	(graded Hilbert–Serre rationality). It wraps the existing Mathlib homogeneous-submodule
218	scaffold (`Submodule.IsHomogeneous`, `DirectSum.Decomposition`, `GradedRing`,
219	`QuotSMulTop`) with the induced gradings on the derived objects (homogeneous submodule,
220	quotient module, quotient ring) that Mathlib does not supply, together with the
221	degreewise rank–nullity difference identity. Blueprint: `subsec:gradedModuleApi`
222	(G1–G5, D5). -/
223	
224	namespace GradedModule
225	
226	section G1
227	
228	variable {R M ι : Type*} [Semiring R] [AddCommMonoid M] [Module R M] [DecidableEq ι]
229	variable (ℳ : ι → Submodule R M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]
230	
231	/-- **G1(a) — independence of the graded pieces of a homogeneous submodule.** The graded
232	pieces `ℳ i ⊓ p` of any submodule `p` are independent, since they refine the independent
233	grading family `ℳ` of the ambient module. Project-local: half of the induced internal
234	direct sum decomposition of a homogeneous submodule. -/
235	theorem homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep (p : Submodule R M) :
236	    iSupIndep fun i => ℳ i ⊓ p :=
237	  ((DirectSum.Decomposition.isInternal ℳ).submodule_iSupIndep).mono fun _ => inf_le_left
238	
239	/-- **G1(b) — a homogeneous submodule is the supremum of its graded pieces.** For an
240	internally graded module `M = ⨁ ℳ i` and a homogeneous submodule `p`
241	(`Submodule.IsHomogeneous`), `p = ⨆ i, (ℳ i ⊓ p)`. Combined with
242	`homogeneousSubmodule_inf_iSupIndep` this exhibits the induced internal direct sum grading
243	`p = ⨁ i, (ℳ i ⊓ p)` that Mathlib's `HomogeneousSubmodule` scaffold does not supply; it
244	gives the graded kernel `K = ker(x : M → M(1))` its grading
245	`K_n = ker(x : M_n → M_{n+1})`.
246	
247	Stated in the ambient `M` (independence + supremum) rather than as
248	`DirectSum.IsInternal` on the subtype `↥p`: the latter triggers a runaway `isDefEq`
249	reduction of `DirectSum.coeLinearMap` over a subtype module. Project-local: the homogeneity
250	input is `Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff`. -/
251	theorem homogeneousSubmodule_iSup_inf_eq (p : Submodule R M) (hp : p.IsHomogeneous ℳ) :
252	    ⨆ i, (ℳ i ⊓ p) = p := by
253	  letI : ∀ (i : ι) (x : ℳ i), Decidable (x ≠ 0) := fun _ _ => Classical.dec _
254	  apply le_antisymm
255	  · exact iSup_le fun i => inf_le_right
256	  · intro x hx
257	    rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose ℳ x]
258	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ fun i _ => Submodule.mem_iSup_of_mem i ?_
259	    exact Submodule.mem_inf.mpr
260	      ⟨SetLike.coe_mem (DirectSum.decompose ℳ x i),
261	        (Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hp).mp hx i⟩
262	
263	end G1
264	
265	/-- **D5 — degreewise rank–nullity difference.** For a `κ`-linear map `φ : V → W`
266	between finite-dimensional `κ`-vector spaces,
267	`dim W − dim V = dim (W ⧸ range φ) − dim (ker φ)` (integer-valued). Applied
268	degreewise with `φ = (x : M_n → M_{n+1})` this is the `hdiff` hypothesis consumed by
269	`AlgebraicGeometry.IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq`. Pure linear algebra — no graded structure used.
270	Project-local: Mathlib has the two halves (`LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker`,
271	`Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank`) but not this packaged difference. -/
272	theorem degreewise_finrank_diff {κ V W : Type*} [Field κ]
273	    [AddCommGroup V] [Module κ V] [FiniteDimensional κ V]
274	    [AddCommGroup W] [Module κ W] [FiniteDimensional κ W]
275	    (φ : V →ₗ[κ] W) :
276	    (Module.finrank κ W : ℤ) - Module.finrank κ V
277	      = (Module.finrank κ (W ⧸ LinearMap.range φ) : ℤ)
278	        - Module.finrank κ (LinearMap.ker φ) := by
279	  have hrn := LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker φ
280	  have hq := Submodule.finrank_quotient_add_finrank (LinearMap.range φ)
281	  omega
282	
283	/-! ### Ambient subquotient induction for Stacks 00K1
284	
285	The Route-2 graded-module side of the inductive step. Everything is phrased over a
286	**fixed** ambient graded `κ`-module `M = ⨁ ℳ n`: a subquotient is a pair of homogeneous
287	submodules `N' ≤ N ⊆ M`, and its Hilbert function is the ambient dimension difference
288	`n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)`. The kernel and cokernel of a degree-one
289	endomorphism are again ambient subquotient pairs, so no `DirectSum.Decomposition` on a
290	quotient/subtype carrier is ever formed (cf.
291	`memory/graded-quotient-module-isdefeq-pathology.md`). -/
292	
293	section Subquotient
294	
295	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
296	variable (ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]
297	
298	/-- A `κ`-linear endomorphism `x` of `M` **raises degree by one** with respect to the
299	grading `ℳ` when `x (ℳ n) ⊆ ℳ (n+1)` for every `n`. This is the abstract, graded-ring-free
300	form of "multiplication by a degree-one element" used in the Stacks 00K1 induction.
301	Project-local. -/
302	def RaisesDegree (x : M →ₗ[κ] M) : Prop := ∀ n, (ℳ n).map x ≤ ℳ (n + 1)
303	
304	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
305	/-- Membership form of `RaisesDegree`. -/
306	lemma RaisesDegree.mem {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x) {n : ℕ} {m : M}
307	    (hm : m ∈ ℳ n) : x m ∈ ℳ (n + 1) :=
308	  hx n (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm)
309	
310	/-- The **ambient subquotient Hilbert function** of a pair of homogeneous submodules
311	`N' ≤ N ⊆ M`: `n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)` (computed in `ℤ`, cast to `ℚ`).
312	This is the data the Stacks 00K1 induction tracks; it depends only on the ambient
313	intersections `N ⊓ ℳ n`, `N' ⊓ ℳ n` of submodules of the fixed `M`. Project-local. -/
314	noncomputable def subquotientHilb (N N' : Submodule κ M) (n : ℕ) : ℚ :=
315	  (((Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) - (Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ n) : ℤ) : ℤ) : ℚ)
316	
317	/-- A degree-raising endomorphism shifts the homogeneous decomposition: the degree-`(i+1)`
318	component of `x m` is `x` applied to the degree-`i` component of `m`. This is the ambient
319	commutation fact that makes preimages and images of homogeneous submodules under `x`
320	homogeneous. Project-local. -/
321	lemma decompose_raisesDegree {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x) (m : M) (i : ℕ) :
322	    (DirectSum.decompose ℳ (x m) (i + 1) : M) = x (DirectSum.decompose ℳ m i) := by
323	  classical
324	  conv_lhs => rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose ℳ m, map_sum, DirectSum.decompose_sum]
325	  simp only [DirectSum.sum_apply, AddSubmonoidClass.coe_finset_sum]
326	  rw [Finset.sum_eq_single i]
327	  · exact DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_same ℳ
328	      (hx i (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (SetLike.coe_mem _)))
329	  · intro j _ hji
330	    rw [DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_ne ℳ
331	      (hx j (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (SetLike.coe_mem _))) (by omega : j + 1 ≠ i + 1)]
332	  · intro hi
333	    simp [DFinsupp.notMem_support_iff.mp hi]
334	
335	/-- The preimage of a homogeneous submodule under a degree-raising endomorphism is
336	homogeneous. Project-local. -/
337	lemma comap_isHomogeneous {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
338	    {N' : Submodule κ M} (hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ) :
339	    (N'.comap x).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
340	  intro i z hz
341	  rw [Submodule.mem_comap, ← decompose_raisesDegree ℳ hx z i]
342	  exact (Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hN').mp (Submodule.mem_comap.mp hz) (i + 1)
343	
344	/-- A degree-raising endomorphism kills the degree-zero component: `x m` has no degree-`0`
345	part. Project-local. -/
346	lemma decompose_raisesDegree_zero {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x) (m : M) :
347	    (DirectSum.decompose ℳ (x m) 0 : M) = 0 := by
348	  classical
349	  conv_lhs => rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose ℳ m, map_sum, DirectSum.decompose_sum]
350	  simp only [DirectSum.sum_apply, AddSubmonoidClass.coe_finset_sum]
351	  refine Finset.sum_eq_zero fun j _ => ?_
352	  rw [DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_ne ℳ
353	    (hx j (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (SetLike.coe_mem _))) (by omega : j + 1 ≠ 0)]
354	
355	/-- The image of a homogeneous submodule under a degree-raising endomorphism is
356	homogeneous. Project-local. -/
357	lemma map_isHomogeneous {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
358	    {N : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) :
359	    (N.map x).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
360	  intro i z hz
361	  obtain ⟨m, hm, rfl⟩ := hz
362	  cases i with
363	  | zero => rw [decompose_raisesDegree_zero ℳ hx m]; exact Submodule.zero_mem _
364	  | succ i =>
365	      rw [decompose_raisesDegree ℳ hx m i]
366	      exact Submodule.mem_map_of_mem ((Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hN).mp hm i)
367	
368	/-- **Ambient image identity.** For a homogeneous submodule `N` and a degree-raising
369	endomorphism `x`, the degree-`(n+1)` part of `x · N` is `x · (N ⊓ ℳ n)`. Project-local. -/
370	lemma map_inf_degree_eq {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
371	    {N : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (n : ℕ) :
372	    N.map x ⊓ ℳ (n + 1) = (N ⊓ ℳ n).map x := by
373	  apply le_antisymm
374	  · rintro y ⟨hy1, hy2⟩
375	    obtain ⟨m, hm, rfl⟩ := hy1
376	    refine Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨DirectSum.decompose ℳ m n, ?_, ?_⟩
377	    · exact Submodule.mem_inf.mpr
378	        ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hN).mp hm n, SetLike.coe_mem _⟩
379	    · rw [← decompose_raisesDegree ℳ hx m n]
380	      exact DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_same ℳ hy2
381	  · refine le_inf (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_left) ?_
382	    rw [Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap]
383	    exact fun m hm => hx.mem ℳ (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hm).2
384	
385	/-- **Ambient distributive law.** Intersecting a sum of two homogeneous submodules with a
386	graded piece distributes: `(P + Q) ⊓ ℳ k = (P ⊓ ℳ k) + (Q ⊓ ℳ k)`. Project-local. -/
387	lemma sup_inf_degree_eq {P Q : Submodule κ M}
388	    (hP : P.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (hQ : Q.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (k : ℕ) :
389	    (P ⊔ Q) ⊓ ℳ k = (P ⊓ ℳ k) ⊔ (Q ⊓ ℳ k) := by
390	  apply le_antisymm
391	  · rintro z ⟨hzPQ, hzk⟩
392	    obtain ⟨p, hp, q, hq, rfl⟩ := Submodule.mem_sup.mp hzPQ
393	    have hpk : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ p k : M) ∈ P ⊓ ℳ k :=
394	      Submodule.mem_inf.mpr ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hP).mp hp k, SetLike.coe_mem _⟩
395	    have hqk : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ q k : M) ∈ Q ⊓ ℳ k :=
396	      Submodule.mem_inf.mpr ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hQ).mp hq k, SetLike.coe_mem _⟩
397	    have hsum : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ p k : M) + (DirectSum.decompose ℳ q k : M) = p + q := by
398	      have h := DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_same ℳ hzk
399	      rw [DirectSum.decompose_add, DirectSum.add_apply] at h
400	      simpa using h
401	    exact hsum ▸ Submodule.add_mem_sup hpk hqk
402	  · exact sup_le (le_inf (inf_le_left.trans le_sup_left) inf_le_right)
403	      (le_inf (inf_le_left.trans le_sup_right) inf_le_right)
404	
405	/-- The intersection of two homogeneous submodules is homogeneous. Project-local: Mathlib
406	provides no lattice-closure lemmas for `Submodule.IsHomogeneous`. -/
407	lemma inf_isHomogeneous {p q : Submodule κ M} (hp : p.IsHomogeneous ℳ)
408	    (hq : q.IsHomogeneous ℳ) : (p ⊓ q).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
409	  intro i z hz
410	  exact Submodule.mem_inf.mpr
411	    ⟨(Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hp).mp (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hz).1 i,
412	      (Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hq).mp (Submodule.mem_inf.mp hz).2 i⟩
413	
414	/-- The sum (supremum) of two homogeneous submodules is homogeneous. Project-local. -/
415	lemma sup_isHomogeneous {p q : Submodule κ M} (hp : p.IsHomogeneous ℳ)
416	    (hq : q.IsHomogeneous ℳ) : (p ⊔ q).IsHomogeneous ℳ := by
417	  intro i z hz
418	  obtain ⟨a, ha, b, hb, rfl⟩ := Submodule.mem_sup.mp hz
419	  have hcoe : (DirectSum.decompose ℳ (a + b) i : M)
420	      = ↑(DirectSum.decompose ℳ a i) + ↑(DirectSum.decompose ℳ b i) := by
421	    rw [DirectSum.decompose_add, DirectSum.add_apply]; rfl
422	  rw [hcoe]
423	  exact Submodule.add_mem_sup ((Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hp).mp ha i)
424	    ((Submodule.IsHomogeneous.mem_iff ℳ hq).mp hb i)
425	
426	/-! #### Kernel/cokernel subquotient building blocks
427	
428	For a degree-raising endomorphism `x` and a homogeneous pair `N' ≤ N`, the kernel
429	subquotient is the pair `(N ⊓ x⁻¹N', N')` and the cokernel subquotient is the pair
430	`(N, N' + x·N)`. The lemmas here record that both new pairs are homogeneous, nest correctly,
431	are annihilated by `x`, and are preserved by any endomorphism `t` commuting with `x` that
432	preserves the original pair — the ambient (carrier-free) content of
433	`lem:graded_subquotient_ker_coker`. -/
434	
435	/-- The kernel subquotient's lower module `N ⊓ x⁻¹N'` is homogeneous. Project-local. -/
436	lemma ker_isHomogeneous {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
437	    {N N' : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ) :
438	    (N ⊓ N'.comap x).IsHomogeneous ℳ :=
439	  inf_isHomogeneous ℳ hN (comap_isHomogeneous ℳ hx hN')
440	
441	/-- The cokernel subquotient's upper module `N' ⊔ x·N` is homogeneous. Project-local. -/
442	lemma coker_isHomogeneous {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
443	    {N N' : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ) :
444	    (N' ⊔ N.map x).IsHomogeneous ℳ :=
445	  sup_isHomogeneous ℳ hN' (map_isHomogeneous ℳ hx hN)
446	
447	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
448	/-- The kernel subquotient nests: `N' ≤ N ⊓ x⁻¹N'`, using `N' ≤ N` and that `x` preserves
449	`N'`. Project-local. -/
450	lemma ker_le {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} {N N' : Submodule κ M} (hle : N' ≤ N)
451	    (hpresN' : N'.map x ≤ N') : N' ≤ N ⊓ N'.comap x :=
452	  le_inf hle (Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap.mp hpresN')
453	
454	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
455	/-- The cokernel subquotient nests: `N' ⊔ x·N ≤ N`, using `N' ≤ N` and that `x` preserves
456	`N`. Project-local. -/
457	lemma coker_le {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} {N N' : Submodule κ M} (hle : N' ≤ N)
458	    (hpresN : N.map x ≤ N) : N' ⊔ N.map x ≤ N :=
459	  sup_le hle hpresN
460	
461	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
462	/-- `x` annihilates the kernel subquotient: `x·(N ⊓ x⁻¹N') ≤ N'`. Project-local. -/
463	lemma ker_annihilate {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} {N N' : Submodule κ M} :
464	    (N ⊓ N'.comap x).map x ≤ N' :=
465	  Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap.mpr inf_le_right
466	
467	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
468	/-- `x` annihilates the cokernel subquotient: `x·N ≤ N' ⊔ x·N`. Project-local. -/
469	lemma coker_annihilate {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} {N N' : Submodule κ M} :
470	    N.map x ≤ N' ⊔ N.map x :=
471	  le_sup_right
472	
473	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
474	/-- An endomorphism `t` commuting with `x` and preserving `N'` preserves the preimage
475	`x⁻¹N'`. Project-local. -/
476	lemma comap_map_le_of_commute {x t : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hcomm : Commute x t)
477	    {N' : Submodule κ M} (ht' : N'.map t ≤ N') :
478	    (N'.comap x).map t ≤ N'.comap x := by
479	  rw [Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap]
480	  intro m hm
481	  rw [Submodule.mem_comap] at hm
482	  rw [Submodule.mem_comap, Submodule.mem_comap]
483	  have key : x (t m) = t (x m) := LinearMap.congr_fun hcomm.eq m
484	  rw [key]
485	  exact ht' (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm)
486	
487	omit [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ] in
488	/-- An endomorphism `t` commuting with `x` and preserving `N` preserves the image `x·N`.
489	Project-local. -/
490	lemma map_map_le_of_commute {x t : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hcomm : Commute x t)
491	    {N : Submodule κ M} (htN : N.map t ≤ N) :
492	    (N.map x).map t ≤ N.map x := by
493	  rw [Submodule.map_le_iff_le_comap]
494	  rintro y ⟨m, hm, rfl⟩
495	  rw [Submodule.mem_comap]
496	  have key : t (x m) = x (t m) := (LinearMap.congr_fun hcomm.eq m).symm
497	  rw [key]
498	  exact Submodule.mem_map_of_mem (htN (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
499	
500	/-- The dimension of the preimage of `S` under the inclusion of a submodule `p` equals the
501	dimension of the ambient intersection `p ⊓ S`. Project-local helper for the degreewise
502	difference identity. -/
503	lemma finrank_comap_subtype (p S : Submodule κ M) :
504	    Module.finrank κ ↥(Submodule.comap p.subtype S) = Module.finrank κ ↥(p ⊓ S) := by
505	  rw [← Submodule.map_comap_subtype p S]
506	  exact (Submodule.equivMapOfInjective p.subtype p.injective_subtype _).finrank_eq
507	
508	variable [∀ n, FiniteDimensional κ ↥(ℳ n)]
509	
510	/-- **D6 — subquotient degreewise difference.** For a degree-raising endomorphism `x` and
511	homogeneous submodules `N`, `N'`, the first difference of the ambient subquotient Hilbert
512	function of `(N, N')` equals the alternating sum of the Hilbert functions of the cokernel
513	subquotient `C = (N, N' ⊔ x·N)` and kernel subquotient `K = (N ⊓ x⁻¹N', N')`:
514	`hilb(n+1) − hilb(n) = hilb_C(n+1) − hilb_K(n)`. This is the `hdiff` hypothesis consumed by
515	`IsRatHilb.ofDiffEq` in the Stacks 00K1 induction. Project-local. -/
516	lemma subquotient_degreewise_diff {x : M →ₗ[κ] M} (hx : RaisesDegree ℳ x)
517	    {N N' : Submodule κ M} (hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ) (n : ℕ) :
518	    subquotientHilb ℳ N N' (n + 1) - subquotientHilb ℳ N N' n
519	      = subquotientHilb ℳ N (N' ⊔ N.map x) (n + 1)
520	        - subquotientHilb ℳ (N ⊓ N'.comap x) N' n := by
521	  classical
522	  haveI : FiniteDimensional κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) := Submodule.finiteDimensional_of_le inf_le_right
523	  haveI : FiniteDimensional κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) := Submodule.finiteDimensional_of_le inf_le_right
524	  set T : Submodule κ M := (N ⊓ ℳ n).map x with hTdef
525	  have hT : N.map x ⊓ ℳ (n + 1) = T := map_inf_degree_eq ℳ hx hN n
526	  have hTle : T ≤ ℳ (n + 1) := hT ▸ inf_le_right
527	  haveI : FiniteDimensional κ ↥T := Submodule.finiteDimensional_of_le hTle
528	  -- the two linear maps into `M ⧸ N'`
529	  set φ : ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) →ₗ[κ] (M ⧸ N') := (N'.mkQ).comp (x.comp (N ⊓ ℳ n).subtype) with hφ
530	  set g : ↥T →ₗ[κ] (M ⧸ N') := (N'.mkQ).comp T.subtype with hg
531	  have hrange : LinearMap.range φ = LinearMap.range g := by
532	    rw [hφ, hg, LinearMap.range_comp, LinearMap.range_comp, LinearMap.range_comp,
533	      Submodule.range_subtype, Submodule.range_subtype, hTdef]
534	  have hkerφ : Module.finrank κ ↥(LinearMap.ker φ)
535	      = Module.finrank κ ↥((N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n) := by
536	    have hk : LinearMap.ker φ = Submodule.comap (N ⊓ ℳ n).subtype (N'.comap x) := by
537	      rw [hφ, LinearMap.ker_comp, Submodule.ker_mkQ, Submodule.comap_comp]
538	    have heq : (N ⊓ ℳ n) ⊓ N'.comap x = (N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n := inf_right_comm _ _ _
539	    rw [hk, finrank_comap_subtype, heq]
540	  have hkerg : Module.finrank κ ↥(LinearMap.ker g) = Module.finrank κ ↥(T ⊓ N') := by
541	    rw [hg, LinearMap.ker_comp, Submodule.ker_mkQ, finrank_comap_subtype]
542	  -- inclusion–exclusion linking the cokernel block to `b` and `T`
543	  have hC : (N' ⊔ N.map x) ⊓ ℳ (n + 1) = (N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) ⊔ T := by
544	    rw [sup_inf_degree_eq ℳ hN' (map_isHomogeneous ℳ hx hN), hT]
545	  have hinf : (N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) ⊓ T = T ⊓ N' := by
546	    rw [inf_right_comm, inf_of_le_left (le_trans inf_le_right hTle), inf_comm]
547	  have hIE := Submodule.finrank_sup_add_finrank_inf_eq (N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) T
548	  rw [← hC, hinf] at hIE
549	  -- the two rank–nullity identities
550	  have RN := LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker φ
551	  have RG := LinearMap.finrank_range_add_finrank_ker g
552	  rw [hkerφ] at RN
553	  rw [hkerg, ← hrange] at RG
554	  -- the integer dimension identity
555	  have key : Module.finrank κ ↥((N' ⊔ N.map x) ⊓ ℳ (n + 1))
556	              + Module.finrank κ ↥((N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n)
557	           = Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n)
558	              + Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) := by
559	    omega
560	  -- assemble
561	  simp only [subquotientHilb]
562	  push_cast
563	  have keyQ : (Module.finrank κ ↥((N' ⊔ N.map x) ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) : ℚ)
564	              + Module.finrank κ ↥((N ⊓ N'.comap x) ⊓ ℳ n)
565	           = Module.finrank κ ↥(N ⊓ ℳ n) + Module.finrank κ ↥(N' ⊓ ℳ (n + 1)) := by
566	    exact_mod_cast key
567	  linarith [keyQ]
568	
569	end Subquotient
570	
571	/-! ### Polynomial-module structure from commuting endomorphisms
572	
573	A finite family `t : Fin r → End κ M` of pairwise-commuting `κ`-linear endomorphisms makes `M`
574	a module over the **free** polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`, with `X i` acting as
575	`t i`. The free polynomial ring — not the subalgebra `Algebra.adjoin κ (range t)` — is used so
576	that the inductive transfer (`subquotient_finite_transfer`) has the ring surjection
577	`MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ ↠ MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` available; relations among the `t i`
578	inside `End κ M` would obstruct the analogous surjection of subalgebras. -/
579	
580	section PolyModule
581	
582	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
583	
584	/-- The ring homomorphism `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ →+* End κ M` evaluating a polynomial at a
585	pairwise-commuting family `t` of endomorphisms, factored through the commutative subalgebra
586	`Algebra.adjoin κ (range t) ⊆ End κ M` (commutative by `Algebra.isMulCommutative_adjoin`).
587	Project-local: the engine for the `MvPolynomial`-module structure on `M`. -/
588	noncomputable def polyEndHom {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
589	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) :
590	    MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ →+* Module.End κ M :=
591	  letI : IsMulCommutative (Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)) :=
592	    Algebra.isMulCommutative_adjoin κ (by
593	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩ _ ⟨j, rfl⟩; exact (hcomm i j).eq)
594	  letI : CommRing (Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)) := IsMulCommutative.instCommRing
595	  ((Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)).val.toRingHom).comp
596	    (MvPolynomial.aeval
597	      (fun i => (⟨t i, Algebra.subset_adjoin (Set.mem_range_self i)⟩ :
598	        Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)))).toRingHom
599	
600	/-- The evaluation ring hom sends the variable `X i` to the endomorphism `t i`. -/
601	@[simp] lemma polyEndHom_X {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
602	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (i : Fin r) :
603	    polyEndHom t hcomm (MvPolynomial.X i) = t i := by
604	  letI : IsMulCommutative (Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)) :=
605	    Algebra.isMulCommutative_adjoin κ (by
606	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩ _ ⟨j, rfl⟩; exact (hcomm i j).eq)
607	  letI : CommRing (Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)) := IsMulCommutative.instCommRing
608	  simp [polyEndHom]
609	
610	/-- The evaluation ring hom sends a constant `C c` to the scalar endomorphism `c • 1`. -/
611	@[simp] lemma polyEndHom_C {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
612	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (c : κ) :
613	    polyEndHom t hcomm (MvPolynomial.C c) = c • (1 : Module.End κ M) := by
614	  letI : IsMulCommutative (Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)) :=
615	    Algebra.isMulCommutative_adjoin κ (by
616	      rintro _ ⟨i, rfl⟩ _ ⟨j, rfl⟩; exact (hcomm i j).eq)
617	  letI : CommRing (Algebra.adjoin κ (Set.range t)) := IsMulCommutative.instCommRing
618	  simp only [polyEndHom, RingHom.comp_apply, AlgHom.toRingHom_eq_coe, RingHom.coe_coe,
619	    MvPolynomial.aeval_C]
620	  rw [Algebra.algebraMap_eq_smul_one]
621	  simp
622	
623	/-- The `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`-module structure on `M` in which `X i` acts as `t i`,
624	obtained by restricting scalars along `polyEndHom`. Project-local: the module over the free
625	polynomial ring required by the ambient subquotient induction. -/
626	noncomputable def polyModule {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
627	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) : Module (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M :=
628	  Module.compHom M (polyEndHom t hcomm)
629	
630	/-- In the polynomial-module structure, `X i` acts as the endomorphism `t i`. -/
631	lemma polyModule_X_smul {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
632	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (i : Fin r) (m : M) :
633	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
634	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) • m = t i m := by
635	  change polyEndHom t hcomm (MvPolynomial.X i) • m = t i m
636	  rw [polyEndHom_X, Module.End.smul_def]
637	
638	/-- In the polynomial-module structure, a constant `C c` acts by the scalar `c`. -/
639	lemma polyModule_C_smul {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
640	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (c : κ) (m : M) :
641	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
642	    (MvPolynomial.C c : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) • m = c • m := by
643	  change polyEndHom t hcomm (MvPolynomial.C c) • m = c • m
644	  rw [polyEndHom_C, Module.End.smul_def]
645	  simp
646	
647	/-- The polynomial-module structure is compatible with the `κ`-action (scalar tower):
648	the algebra map `κ → MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` followed by the polynomial action recovers the
649	original `κ`-action on `M`. -/
650	lemma polyModule_isScalarTower {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
651	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) :
652	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
653	    IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M := by
654	  letI := polyModule t hcomm
655	  refine IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul fun c m => ?_
656	  change polyEndHom t hcomm (algebraMap κ (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) c) • m = c • m
657	  rw [MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq, polyEndHom_C, Module.End.smul_def]
658	  simp
659	
660	/-- A `κ`-submodule `N` that is stable under each commuting endomorphism `t i` is a
661	`MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`-submodule of `M` (same carrier), for the polynomial-module structure
662	`polyModule`. Project-local: lifts an ambient `t`-stable submodule to the polynomial ring,
663	keeping every carrier an ambient submodule of `M` (no derived carrier). -/
664	noncomputable def polySubmodule {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
665	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (N : Submodule κ M)
666	    (hN : ∀ i, N.map (t i) ≤ N) :
667	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
668	    Submodule (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M :=
669	  letI := polyModule t hcomm
670	  { carrier := N
671	    add_mem' := fun ha hb => N.add_mem ha hb
672	    zero_mem' := N.zero_mem
673	    smul_mem' := by
674	      have key : ∀ (p : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ), ∀ m ∈ N, p • m ∈ N := by
675	        intro p
676	        induction p using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
677	        | C a => intro m hm; rw [polyModule_C_smul]; exact N.smul_mem a hm
678	        | add p q hp hq => intro m hm; rw [add_smul]; exact N.add_mem (hp m hm) (hq m hm)
679	        | mul_X p i hp =>
680	            intro m hm
681	            rw [mul_smul, polyModule_X_smul]
682	            exact hp _ (hN i (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))
683	      intro p m hm
684	      exact key p m hm }
685	
686	/-- The carrier of `polySubmodule` is the original `κ`-submodule. -/
687	@[simp] lemma polySubmodule_coe {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
688	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) (N : Submodule κ M)
689	    (hN : ∀ i, N.map (t i) ≤ N) :
690	    letI := polyModule t hcomm
691	    ((polySubmodule t hcomm N hN : Submodule (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) M) : Set M) = N :=
692	  rfl
693	
694	end PolyModule
695	
696	/-! ### The last-variable surjection of polynomial rings
697	
698	The finiteness transfer of the ambient subquotient induction factors the action of
699	`MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ` on a subquotient annihilated by the last endomorphism through the
700	free polynomial ring on one fewer variable, `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`, via the surjection
701	`X (Fin.last r) ↦ 0`, `X (Fin.castSucc i) ↦ X i`. -/
702	
703	section LastVar
704	
705	variable {κ : Type*} [Field κ]
706	
707	/-- The `κ`-algebra surjection `MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ ↠ MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` sending the
708	last variable `X (Fin.last r)` to `0` and `X (Fin.castSucc i)` to `X i`. Project-local: the ring
709	surjection along which the finiteness transfer factors
710	(`lem:fg_restrictScalars_of_surjective_mathlib`). -/
711	noncomputable def lastVarAlgHom (r : ℕ) (κ : Type*) [Field κ] :
712	    MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ →ₐ[κ] MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ :=
713	  MvPolynomial.aeval (Fin.lastCases 0 (fun i => MvPolynomial.X i))
714	
715	@[simp] lemma lastVarAlgHom_X_castSucc (r : ℕ) (i : Fin r) :
716	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.X (Fin.castSucc i)) = MvPolynomial.X i := by
717	  simp [lastVarAlgHom]
718	
719	@[simp] lemma lastVarAlgHom_X_last (r : ℕ) :
720	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.X (Fin.last r)) = 0 := by
721	  simp [lastVarAlgHom]
722	
723	@[simp] lemma lastVarAlgHom_C (r : ℕ) (c : κ) :
724	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.C c) = MvPolynomial.C c := by
725	  simp [lastVarAlgHom]
726	
727	/-- `lastVarAlgHom` is a left inverse of `rename Fin.castSucc`, hence surjective. -/
728	lemma lastVarAlgHom_rename_castSucc (r : ℕ) (q : MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ) :
729	    lastVarAlgHom r κ (MvPolynomial.rename Fin.castSucc q) = q := by
730	  induction q using MvPolynomial.induction_on with
731	  | C a => simp
732	  | add p q hp hq => simp [hp, hq]
733	  | mul_X p i hp => simp [hp]
734	
735	lemma lastVarAlgHom_surjective (r : ℕ) : Function.Surjective (lastVarAlgHom r κ) :=
736	  fun q => ⟨MvPolynomial.rename Fin.castSucc q, lastVarAlgHom_rename_castSucc r q⟩
737	
738	instance lastVarAlgHom_ringHomSurjective (r : ℕ) :
739	    RingHomSurjective (lastVarAlgHom r κ).toRingHom :=
740	  ⟨lastVarAlgHom_surjective r⟩
741	
742	end LastVar
743	
744	/-! ### Finiteness transfer down one variable
745	
746	The keystone of the ambient subquotient induction: if a subquotient is finite over the free
747	polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin (r+1)) κ` and the last endomorphism annihilates it, then it is
748	finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`. The action factors through the `lastVarAlgHom` surjection. -/
749	
750	section Transfer
751	
752	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
753	
754	/-- A `t`-stable submodule `P'` is closed under the action of any polynomial via `polyEndHom`. -/
755	lemma polyEndHom_mem_of_stable {r : ℕ} (t : Fin r → Module.End κ M)
756	    (hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)) {P' : Submodule κ M}

774	    (hP : ∀ i, P.map (t i) ≤ P) (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P')

778	      - (polyEndHom (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _)
779	          (lastVarAlgHom r κ s)) m ∈ P' := by

781	  | C a =>

790	      abel
791	  | mul_X p j hp =>
792	      intro m hm
793	      rw [map_mul, polyEndHom_X, Module.End.mul_apply]
794	      rcases Fin.eq_castSucc_or_eq_last j with ⟨i, rfl⟩ | rfl

799	        rw [map_mul, lastVarAlgHom_X_last, mul_zero, map_zero, LinearMap.zero_apply, sub_zero]

801	          (hannih (Submodule.mem_map_of_mem hm))

803	/-- **Finiteness transfer down one variable (core).** If the subquotient `P/P'` (carriers

808	lemma subquotient_finite_transfer {r : ℕ} (t : Fin (r + 1) → Module.End κ M)

810	    (hP : ∀ i, P.map (t i) ≤ P) (hP' : ∀ i, P'.map (t i) ≤ P')

812	    (hpar : letI := polyModule t hcomm
813	      Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin (r + 1)) κ)
814	        (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm P hP) ⧸
815	          (polySubmodule t hcomm P' hP').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm P hP).subtype)) :
816	    letI := polyModule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _)
817	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
818	      (↥(polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P
819	            (fun i => hP _)) ⧸
820	        (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P'
821	            (fun i => hP' _)).comap
822	          (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P
823	            (fun i => hP _)).subtype) := by

827	  haveI := hpar

829	    (lastVarAlgHom r κ).toRingHom with hσ

831	  set g : ↥(polySubmodule t hcomm P hP) →ₛₗ[σ]
832	      (↥(polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P (fun i => hP _)) ⧸
833	        (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P'
834	            (fun i => hP' _)).comap
835	          (polySubmodule (fun i => t (Fin.castSucc i)) (fun i j => hcomm _ _) P
836	            (fun i => hP _)).subtype) :=
837	    { toFun := fun y => Submodule.Quotient.mk ⟨(y : M), y.2⟩
838	      map_add' := fun a b => by rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_add]; rfl
839	      map_smul' := by
840	        intro s y
841	        rw [← Submodule.Quotient.mk_smul, Submodule.Quotient.eq, Submodule.mem_comap]
842	        change (polyEndHom t hcomm s) (y : M)

847	    intro z

849	    exact ⟨⟨(y : M), y.2⟩, rfl⟩

932	/-! ### The ambient subquotient datum
933	
934	Bundles a homogeneous pair `N' ≤ N` of a fixed graded `κ`-module `M = ⨁ ℳ n` with `r`
935	pairwise-commuting degree-raising endomorphisms preserving the pair, plus the finiteness of
936	the represented subquotient `N/N'` over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` (via
937	`polySubmodule`, so the underlying carriers stay ambient submodules of `M`). This is the
938	length-`r` carrier of the Stacks 00K1 ambient induction (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`). -/
939	
940	section Datum
941	
942	variable {κ M : Type*} [Field κ] [AddCommGroup M] [Module κ M]
943	variable (ℳ : ℕ → Submodule κ M) [DirectSum.Decomposition ℳ]
944	
945	/-- A length-`r` **ambient subquotient datum** over the fixed graded module `M = ⨁ ℳ n`.
946	Project-local: the carrier of the Stacks 00K1 ambient induction (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`).
947	The finiteness field `hfin` records that the represented subquotient `N/N'` is a finite module
948	over the free polynomial ring `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ` acting through the `t i`; the carriers
949	involved are the ambient `t`-stable submodules `polySubmodule … N`, `polySubmodule … N'`. -/
950	structure SubquotientDatum (r : ℕ) where
951	  /-- The upper homogeneous submodule. -/
952	  N : Submodule κ M
953	  /-- The lower homogeneous submodule. -/
954	  N' : Submodule κ M
955	  /-- `N'` is contained in `N`. -/
956	  hle : N' ≤ N
957	  /-- `N` is homogeneous. -/
958	  hN : N.IsHomogeneous ℳ
959	  /-- `N'` is homogeneous. -/
960	  hN' : N'.IsHomogeneous ℳ
961	  /-- The `r` degree-raising endomorphisms. -/
962	  t : Fin r → Module.End κ M
963	  /-- They pairwise commute. -/
964	  hcomm : ∀ i j, Commute (t i) (t j)
965	  /-- Each raises degree by one. -/
966	  hraise : ∀ i, RaisesDegree ℳ (t i)
967	  /-- Each preserves `N`. -/
968	  hpresN : ∀ i, N.map (t i) ≤ N
969	  /-- Each preserves `N'`. -/
970	  hpresN' : ∀ i, N'.map (t i) ≤ N'
971	  /-- The represented subquotient `N/N'` is finite over `MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ`. -/
972	  hfin : letI := polyModule t hcomm
973	    Module.Finite (MvPolynomial (Fin r) κ)
974	      (↥(polySubmodule t hcomm N hpresN) ⧸
975	        (polySubmodule t hcomm N' hpresN').comap (polySubmodule t hcomm N hpresN).subtype)
976	
977	/-- The ambient Hilbert function `n ↦ dim_κ(N ⊓ ℳ n) − dim_κ(N' ⊓ ℳ n)` of a subquotient
978	datum (`def:graded_subquotientHilb`). -/
979	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.hilb {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ r) : ℕ → ℚ :=
980	  subquotientHilb ℳ D.N D.N'
981	
982	/-- The kernel pair's lower module `N ⊓ x⁻¹N'` is stable under every endomorphism of the family
983	(needed for the finiteness transfer over the full polynomial ring). -/
984	lemma ker_stable_full {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) (i : Fin (r + 1)) :
985	    (D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))).map (D.t i)
986	      ≤ D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r)) :=
987	  le_trans (le_inf (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_left) (Submodule.map_mono inf_le_right))
988	    (inf_le_inf (D.hpresN i) (comap_map_le_of_commute (D.hcomm (Fin.last r) i) (D.hpresN' i)))
989	
990	/-- The cokernel pair's upper module `N' ⊔ x·N` is stable under every endomorphism of the family. -/
991	lemma coker_stable_full {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) (i : Fin (r + 1)) :
992	    (D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r))).map (D.t i)
993	      ≤ D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r)) := by
994	  rw [Submodule.map_sup]
995	  exact sup_le_sup (D.hpresN' i)
996	    (map_map_le_of_commute (D.hcomm (Fin.last r) i) (D.hpresN i))
997	
998	/-- **Kernel constructor.** From a length-`(r+1)` subquotient datum, the kernel subquotient
999	`(N ⊓ x⁻¹N', N')` of multiplication by `x = t (last)`, as a length-`r` datum on `t ∘ castSucc`.
1000	All non-finiteness fields are the ambient kernel/cokernel calculus; the finiteness field is the
1001	keystone transfer `subquotient_finite_transfer` (`lem:graded_subquotient_finite_transfer`). -/
1002	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.ker {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) :
1003	    SubquotientDatum ℳ r where
1004	  N := D.N ⊓ (D.N').comap (D.t (Fin.last r))
1005	  N' := D.N'
1006	  hle := ker_le D.hle (D.hpresN' (Fin.last r))
1007	  hN := ker_isHomogeneous ℳ (D.hraise (Fin.last r)) D.hN D.hN'
1008	  hN' := D.hN'
1009	  t := fun i => D.t (Fin.castSucc i)
1010	  hcomm := fun i j => D.hcomm _ _
1011	  hraise := fun i => D.hraise _
1012	  hpresN := fun i => ker_stable_full ℳ D (Fin.castSucc i)
1013	  hpresN' := fun i => D.hpresN' _
1014	  hfin :=
1015	    subquotient_finite_transfer D.t D.hcomm (ker_stable_full ℳ D) D.hpresN'
1016	      ker_annihilate
1017	      (polyQuot_finite_of_le_numerator D.t D.hcomm (ker_stable_full ℳ D) D.hpresN D.hpresN'
1018	        inf_le_left D.hfin)
1019	
1020	/-- **Cokernel constructor.** From a length-`(r+1)` subquotient datum, the cokernel subquotient
1021	`(N, N' ⊔ x·N)` of multiplication by `x = t (last)`, as a length-`r` datum on `t ∘ castSucc`. -/
1022	noncomputable def SubquotientDatum.coker {r : ℕ} (D : SubquotientDatum ℳ (r + 1)) :
1023	    SubquotientDatum ℳ r where
1024	  N := D.N
1025	  N' := D.N' ⊔ D.N.map (D.t (Fin.last r))
1026	  hle := coker_le D.hle (D.hpresN (Fin.last r))
1027	  hN := D.hN
1028	  hN' := coker_isHomogeneous ℳ (D.hraise (Fin.last r)) D.hN D.hN'
1029	  t := fun i => D.t (Fin.castSucc i)
1030	  hcomm := fun i j => D.hcomm _ _
1031	  hraise := fun i => D.hraise _
1032	  hpresN := fun i => D.hpresN _
1033	  hpresN' := fun i => coker_stable_full ℳ D (Fin.castSucc i)
1034	  hfin :=
1035	    subquotient_finite_transfer D.t D.hcomm D.hpresN (coker_stable_full ℳ D)
1036	      coker_annihilate
1037	      (polyQuot_finite_of_le_denominator D.t D.hcomm D.hpresN D.hpresN' (coker_stable_full ℳ D)
1038	        le_sup_left D.hfin)

1040	/-- Base-case finiteness: a module finite over `MvPolynomial σ κ` for an *empty* index `σ` —

1042	since `MvPolynomial σ κ ≃ₐ[κ] κ`. Project-local: the base case of the Stacks 00K1 induction.
1043	Stated outside the `Datum` section as it needs no grading. -/
1044	lemma finiteDimensional_of_mvPolynomial_isEmpty_finite
1045	    {κ : Type*} [Field κ] {σ : Type*} [IsEmpty σ]
1046	    {Q : Type*} [AddCommGroup Q] [Module κ Q]
1047	    [Module (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q] [IsScalarTower κ (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q]
1048	    [Module.Finite (MvPolynomial σ κ) Q] : FiniteDimensional κ Q := by

1050	    Module.Finite.equiv (MvPolynomial.isEmptyAlgEquiv κ σ).symm.toLinearEquiv

1057	/-- **Independence of images modulo the kernel.** If a family `g i` of submodules of `A` is
1058	"independent modulo `ker π`" — every `a ∈ g i` that also lies in `ker π ⊔ ⨆ j ≠ i, g j` already
1059	lies in `ker π` — then the images `π '' (g i)` form an `iSupIndep` family. The image family
1060	inherits the degree-`i` separation of the `g i` once the kernel is quotiented out. Project-local:
1061	the abstract lattice core of the base-case independence
1062	(`lem:graded_subquotient_base_eventuallyZero`, Step 1), kept ring-agnostic so it can be applied to
1063	the `κ`-linear quotient map `↥N ↠ N/N'`. -/
1064	lemma iSupIndep_map_of_mem_ker_sup {F A Q : Type*} [Field F] [AddCommGroup A]
1065	    [Module F A] [AddCommGroup Q] [Module F Q] (π : A →ₗ[F] Q) {ι : Type*} (g : ι → Submodule F A)
1066	    (h : ∀ i, ∀ a ∈ g i, a ∈ LinearMap.ker π ⊔ ⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), g j → a ∈ LinearMap.ker π) :
1067	    iSupIndep (fun i => Submodule.map π (g i)) := by
1068	  rw [iSupIndep_def]
1069	  intro i
1070	  rw [Submodule.disjoint_def]
1071	  intro q hq1 hq2
1072	  obtain ⟨a, ha, rfl⟩ := hq1
1073	  rw [show (⨆ j, ⨆ (_ : j ≠ i), Submodule.map π (g j))

1672	and `F` quasi-coherent, the base-change map `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` is an isomorphism as soon
1673	as it restricts to an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of the base `S'`. This is

1676	the per-affine-open hypothesis being the affine–affine section assertion supplied (over each chart)

1678	carried because the per-open assertion `H` is only available under them (they make `X` restrict to
1679	`Spec A` and `F` to `M^~` over each affine chart); the reduction itself is the locality criterion.
1680	See blueprint `lem:base_change_map_affine_local`. -/
1681	theorem base_change_map_affine_local (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
1682	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
1683	    (H : ∀ U : S'.affineOpens, IsIso ((pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F).app U)) :
1684	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) :=
1685	  (Modules.isIso_iff_isIso_app_affineOpens (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F)).mpr H

1689	affine-local picture this is the associativity isomorphism
1690	`(R' ⊗_R A) ⊗_A M ≅ R' ⊗_R M`, which needs no flatness.
1691	
1692	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma "Affine base change". -/
1693	theorem affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso (h : IsPullback g' f' f g) [IsAffineHom f]
1694	    (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent] :
1695	    IsIso (pushforwardBaseChangeMap f g f' g' h.w F) := by
1696	  -- FIRST REDUCTION (locality on `S'`): by `base_change_map_affine_local` it suffices to check
1697	  -- that the base-change map is an isomorphism on the sections over every affine open of `S'`.
1698	  -- This is exactly the locality reduction; the per-affine-open hypothesis is the affine–affine
1699	  -- section assertion that `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` is intended to supply.
1700	  apply base_change_map_affine_local f g f' g' h F
1701	  intro U

1703	  --
1704	  -- The two obligations of the blueprint affine close are now NAMED declarations:
1705	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL identification is `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange`
1706	  --     (above): in the affine–affine model `Γ(α) = cancelBaseChange⁻¹`, hence an iso (no
1707	  --     flatness). Its statement is in place; its proof (the 4-step generator trace) is the
1708	  --     genuine crux still carrying a `sorry`.
1709	  --   • the SECTION-LEVEL locality reduction is `base_change_map_affine_local` (just applied):
1710	  --     it reduced the global iso to this per-affine-open `U` goal.
1711	  --
1712	  -- WHAT REMAINS HERE (the AFFINE REDUCTION, "obligation 1"): the ambient `S, S', X, X'` are
1713	  -- ARBITRARY (only `f` affine, `F` quasi-coherent). To discharge the per-`U` goal one must
1714	  -- restrict the cartesian square over the affine open `U = Spec R' ⊆ S'` and a chosen affine
1715	  -- `Spec R ⊆ S` containing `g(U)` — over which `[IsAffineHom f]` makes `X` restrict to `Spec A`
1716	  -- and `[F.IsQuasicoherent]` makes `F` restrict to `M^~` — and IDENTIFY `(pushforwardBaseChangeMap
1717	  -- …).app U` with the affine–affine base-change map of the restricted square (blueprint Step 2:
1718	  -- naturality of the adjunction transpose + pushforward-commutes-with-restriction). Then the
1719	  -- per-`U` goal is exactly `pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange` (composed with the

8	/-!
9	# Generic flatness of a coherent sheaf (A.2.a)
10	
11	This file packages the classical Grothendieck/Nitsure~\S4 **generic flatness**
12	theorem: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent `𝓞_X`-module on a
13	finite-type morphism `X ⟶ S` becomes flat after restricting to a non-empty open
14	subscheme of `S`. This is the inductive engine underlying the
15	flattening-stratification construction of Grothendieck's existence theorem for
16	the Quot scheme.
17	
18	Each blueprint-pinned declaration carries the *intended* substantive type
19	signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
20	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`) with a `sorry`
21	body where the proof is not yet supplied. The substantive generic-flatness
22	proof is deep (Nitsure~\S4: prime-filtration dévissage + Noether normalisation +
23	clearing denominators).
24	
25	The blueprint-pinned declaration is:
26	
27	- `AlgebraicGeometry.genericFlatness` (theorem) — **generic flatness**
28	  [Nitsure §4 Theorem]: over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf
29	  on a finite-type `X ⟶ S` is flat over a non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. Its
30	  algebraic form (`thm:generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin) is the
31	  statement that a finite module over a finite-type algebra over a noetherian
32	  domain becomes free after inverting one non-zero element of the base.
33	
34	The file-internal `GenericFreeness` namespace lands the **finite-module /
35	finite-morphism special case** of the algebraic form axiom-clean, as a reusable
36	building block for the dévissage's leaves.
37	
38	## Note on type expressivity
39	
40	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
41	`genericFlatness` requires the existence of a *non-empty* open `V` and
42	substantive flatness on every affine `U ⊆ V`; both quantifiers are necessary
43	(without them the statement collapses to `V = ∅`).
44	
45	## Mathlib status
46	
47	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides `Module.Flat`, `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat`
48	(morphism-level), `IsImmersion`, `IsLocallyNoetherian`, `IsIntegral`,
49	`LocallyOfFiniteType`, and the dévissage / Noether-normalisation infrastructure
50	the algebraic generic-flatness argument consumes. It does NOT yet provide the
51	polynomial-ring core of generic freeness, nor the geometric `genericFlatness`
52	statement packaging it over a noetherian integral base.
53	
54	## References
55	
56	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FlatteningStratification.tex`.
57	Source: Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot schemes", §4 (FGA Explained
58	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–18); Stacks Project tag 00HB (module flat).
59	-/

1930	`B`-module `M` (viewed as an `A`-module through the scalar tower `A → B → M`),
1931	there is a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is free over `A_f`.

1936	  *module-finite over `A`*, this is the finite-module helper
1937	  `GenericFreeness.exists_free_localizationAway_of_finite` — over `Frac A` the

2044	/-! ## Generic flatness (Nitsure §4)
2045	
2046	Over a noetherian integral base `S`, a coherent sheaf on a finite-type
2047	`X ⟶ S` is flat above some non-empty open `V ⊆ S`. This is the inductive
2048	engine of the flattening-stratification theorem: combined with
2049	Noetherian induction on the closed complement `S ∖ V`, it produces the
2050	finite stratification of `S` by flatness loci.
2051	
2052	Algebraically (theorem `generic_flatness_algebraic`, no Lean pin): for a
2053	noetherian domain `A`, a finite-type `A`-algebra `B`, and a finite
2054	`B`-module `M`, there exists a non-zero `f ∈ A` such that `M_f` is a
2055	free `A_f`-module. The geometric form (this declaration) restricts to a
2056	non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` and applies the algebraic form on
2057	each finite-type-algebra patch of `X` above `Spec A`.
2058	
2059	Blueprint reference: `thm:generic_flatness` (Nitsure §4). -/
2060	
2061	/-- **Generic flatness theorem** (Nitsure §4 / Stacks ?).
2062	
2063	For a noetherian integral scheme `S`, a finite-type morphism `p : X ⟶ S`,
2064	and a coherent `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, there exists a non-empty open subscheme
2065	`V ⊆ S` such that `𝓕|_{X_V} = 𝓕|_{p⁻¹V}` is flat over `𝓞_V`.
2066	
2067	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §4: pass to a non-empty affine open
2068	`Spec A ⊆ S` where `A` is a noetherian domain, then apply the algebraic
2069	form (Noether normalisation + Auslander–Buchsbaum-style filtration
2070	argument) to each finite-type-`A`-algebra `B` arising from an affine
2071	cover of `p⁻¹(Spec A)`. The witness `V` is the common basic open
2072	`D(f_1 f_2 ⋯ f_r) ⊆ Spec A` clearing the finitely many
2073	generic-flatness elements `f_i ∈ A` produced on each patch. -/
2074	theorem genericFlatness {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsIntegral S] [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
2075	    (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] (F : X.Modules)
2076	    [F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType] :
2077	    ∃ (V : S.Opens), (V : Set S).Nonempty ∧
2078	      ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) (_ : U ≤ V) {W : X.Opens}
2079	        (_ : IsAffineOpen W) (e : W ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U),
2080	        letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := Module.compHom _ (p.appLE U W e).hom
2081	        Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, W) := by
2082	  -- Geometric assembly of `genericFlatnessAlgebraic` (Nitsure §4, geometric form).
2083	  --
2084	  -- Genuine starting point: `S` is integral, hence non-empty, so it has a
2085	  -- non-empty affine open `Spec A ⊆ S` with `A := Γ(S, U₀)` a noetherian
2086	  -- domain (integrality gives `IsDomain A`; local-noetherianity gives
2087	  -- `IsNoetherianRing A`).
2088	  obtain ⟨x₀⟩ := (IsIntegral.nonempty : Nonempty ↥S)
2089	  obtain ⟨U₀, hU₀aff, hx₀, -⟩ :=
2090	    exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset (x := x₀) (U := ⊤) (by trivial)
2091	  -- `U₀` is a non-empty affine open of `S`; its sections `A := Γ(S, U₀)` are a
2092	  -- noetherian domain (the base of the algebraic generic-flatness input).
2093	  --
2094	  -- Remaining assembly (the genuine geometric content, still `sorry`):
2095	  --   * `p` is locally of finite type and `F` is coherent
2096	  --     (`[F.IsQuasicoherent] [F.IsFiniteType]`), so over each affine open
2097	  --     `W ⊆ p⁻¹(U₀)` the sections `M := Γ(F, W)` form a finite module over the
2098	  --     finite-type `A`-algebra `B := Γ(X, W)`;
2099	  --   * `p⁻¹(U₀)` is quasi-compact (X locally noetherian above the affine), so it
2100	  --     is covered by finitely many such affine `W_j`;
2101	  --   * `genericFlatnessAlgebraic A B_j M_j` yields `f_j ∈ A`, `f_j ≠ 0`, with
2102	  --     `(M_j)_{f_j}` free over `A_{f_j}`;
2103	  --   * set `V := D(∏ⱼ f_j) ⊆ U₀` (non-empty, as `A` is a domain and each
2104	  --     `f_j ≠ 0`); flatness on every affine `U ≤ V`, `W ≤ p⁻¹U` then follows
2105	  --     from freeness over the localisation by flat-locality
2106	  --     (`Module.flat_of_isLocalized_maximal` / `Module.Flat.of_free`).
2107	  -- The witness `V` must come out of this construction: an arbitrary non-empty
2108	  -- open makes the flatness obligation false, so we do not commit to one here.
2109	  sorry
2110	
2111	end AlgebraicGeometry

6	import Mathlib

8	/-!
9	# The Quot scheme: Quot-foundations layer
10	
11	This file packages the Quot-foundations layer of the
12	Grothendieck–Altman–Kleiman Quot-scheme construction. It introduces the
13	per-fiber Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, the Quot functor
14	`Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of `T`-flat coherent quotients, and the Grassmannian
15	*scheme* (Mathlib at the pinned commit carries only a linear-algebra
16	Grassmannian) together with its representability.
17	
18	The 4 blueprint-pinned declarations are:
19	
20	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.hilbertPolynomial` (def) — the
21	   **Hilbert polynomial function** `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]` of a coherent
22	   sheaf `F` on `X` over a finite-type `π : X ⟶ S` with respect to a
23	   line bundle `L`. Encoded as a function `S → Polynomial ℚ`.
24	
25	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (def) — the **Quot
26	   functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` sending an
27	   `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the set of equivalence classes
28	   `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with `F` a `T`-flat coherent sheaf on
29	   `X_T`, `q : E_T ↠ F` a surjection, and `F|_{X_t}` having Hilbert
30	   polynomial `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
31	
32	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (def) — the
33	   **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
34	   rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V`.
35	
36	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian.representable` (theorem)
37	   — the **representability of the Grassmannian** by a smooth projective
38	   `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with
39	   the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
40	
41	## Note on type expressivity
42	
43	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof",
44	each declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
45	
46	- `hilbertPolynomial` returns `Polynomial ℚ` keyed by `s : S`, not
47	  `Unit`; the Hilbert polynomial is a non-trivial invariant of the
48	  coherent sheaf at the fiber over `s`.
49	- `QuotFunctor` and `Grassmannian` return contravariant functors into
50	  `Type u` — substantive presheaves of sets, not constant functors.
51	- `Grassmannian.representable` packages the
52	  `Functor.RepresentableBy` Yoneda-bijection structure: existence of a
53	  scheme `Y` together with a `RepresentableBy Y` witness — substantive
54	  content (a representable functor is determined by its representing
55	  object up to canonical isomorphism, and the witness is the data of
56	  that isomorphism family).
57	
58	## Mathlib status
59	
60	Mathlib (master `b80f227`) provides:
61	- `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules` (the category `X.Modules`),
62	- `Scheme.Modules.pullback`, `Scheme.Modules.pushforward` (the
63	  pullback–pushforward adjunction at level `i = 0`),
64	- `CategoryTheory.Functor.RepresentableBy` for representable functors,
65	- `AlgebraicGeometry.LocallyOfFiniteType`, `AlgebraicGeometry.IsLocallyNoetherian`
66	  (morphism / object property predicates), and
67	- `Polynomial` for `ℚ[λ]`.
68	
69	Mathlib does NOT provide (at the pinned commit):
70	- a Grassmannian *scheme* (only a linear-algebra Grassmannian
71	  as a finite-rank-quotient variety),
72	- the Quot/Hilbert functor or its representability,
73	- Snapper's Lemma for the polynomial property of Euler characteristics.
74	
75	## References
76	
77	Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`. Source:
78	Nitsure, "Construction of Hilbert and Quot Schemes", §1 (FGA Explained
79	Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020 pp. 5–35); cf. Hartshorne III.5.2.
80	-/
81	
82	set_option autoImplicit false
83	
84	universe u
85	
86	open CategoryTheory Limits
87	
88	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
89	
90	namespace Scheme

92	/-! ## §1. Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf
93	
94	For a finite-type morphism `π : X ⟶ S` with `S` noetherian and a coherent
95	sheaf `F` on `X` whose schematic support is proper over `S` (here encoded
96	as plain `X.Modules` for the file-skeleton), the per-fiber Hilbert
97	polynomial is the function
98	
99	`s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ],   Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`.
100	
101	Snapper's Lemma ensures this is a polynomial in `m`; the proof requires
102	graded-Euler-characteristic infrastructure and is not stated here.
103	
104	Blueprint reference: `def:hilbert_polynomial` (Nitsure §1; cf. Hartshorne
105	III.5.2). -/
106	
107	/-- The **Hilbert polynomial** of a coherent sheaf `F` on `X` over `S` at
108	the fiber over `s ∈ S` with respect to a line bundle `L` on `X`.
109	
110	Encoded as a function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s} ∈ ℚ[λ]`. The defining formula
111	
112	`Φ_{F,s}(m) = χ(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗ m})
113	            = Σ_i (-1)^i dim_{κ(s)} H^i(X_s, F|_{X_s} ⊗ L_s^{⊗m})`
114	
115	is a polynomial in `m` by Snapper's Lemma; the polynomial coefficients
116	depend on `s` through the fiber `F|_{X_s}`. When `F` is `S`-flat the
117	function `s ↦ Φ_{F,s}` is locally constant on `S`.
118	
119	iter-177+: the body unfolds to the graded-Euler-characteristic
120	construction once `χ` of a coherent sheaf on a noetherian scheme +
121	Snapper's polynomial-eventually-property are in scope. For the iter-176
122	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
123	noncomputable def hilbertPolynomial {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
124	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _F : X.Modules) (_s : S) :
125	    Polynomial ℚ :=
126	  sorry
127	
128	/-! ## §2. The Quot functor
129	
130	The Quot functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` sends an `S`-scheme `T ⟶ S` to the
131	set of equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` where
132	- `F` is a coherent sheaf on `X_T = X ×_S T` whose schematic support is
133	  proper over `T` and which is `T`-flat,
134	- `q : E_T ↠ F` is a surjective `O_{X_T}`-linear homomorphism,
135	- the fiberwise Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` with respect to `L|_{X_t}`
136	  equals `Φ` at every `t ∈ T`.
137	
138	Two pairs `(F, q)` and `(F', q')` are equivalent iff `ker(q) = ker(q')`.
139	
140	The Hilbert scheme is the special case `E = O_X`:
141	`Hilb^{Φ,L}_{X/S} = Quot^{Φ,L}_{O_X/X/S}`.
142	
143	Blueprint reference: `def:quot_functor` (Nitsure §1; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
144	
145	/-- The **Quot functor** `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S}` of coherent quotients of `E`
146	on `X ×_S -` with Hilbert polynomial `Φ`.
147	
148	Encoded as a contravariant functor `(Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u`, sending an
149	`S`-scheme `T → S` (i.e. an object of `Over S`) to the set of
150	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` of a `T`-flat coherent
151	sheaf `F` on `X ×_S T` with proper support and a surjection
152	`q : E_T ↠ F` whose fiberwise Hilbert polynomial is `Φ`, modulo
153	`ker(q) = ker(q')`. Functoriality is pullback of the quotient along
154	`X ×_S T' ⟶ X ×_S T`.
155	
156	iter-177+: the body packages the on-objects / on-morphisms data using the
157	`Scheme.Modules.pullback` bifunctor on the relative product
158	`X ×_S T`, with the equivalence relation `ker(q) = ker(q')` quotiented
159	out via `Setoid` / `Quotient`. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body
160	is a typed `sorry`. -/
161	noncomputable def QuotFunctor {S X : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
162	    (_π : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType _π] (_L _E : X.Modules)
163	    (_Φ : Polynomial ℚ) :
164	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
165	  sorry
166	
167	end Scheme
168	
169	/-! ## §3. The Grassmannian scheme
170	
171	Since Mathlib carries no Grassmannian *scheme*, we encode it here as a
172	contravariant functor on `Over S` together with a representability
173	statement. The construction proceeds by gluing `binom(r, d)` affine
174	charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the Plücker cocycle, yielding a smooth
175	projective `S`-scheme `Gr_S(V, d)` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`,
176	equipped with a tautological rank-`d` quotient
177	`π* V ↠ U` and the Plücker closed embedding into `ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
178	
179	Blueprint references: `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
180	`thm:grassmannian_representable` (Nitsure §1 Exercise (2),
181	"Construction of Grassmannian"; FGA Explained Ch. 5). -/
182	
183	namespace Scheme
184	
185	/-- The **Grassmannian functor** `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)^op ⥤ Set` of
186	rank-`d` quotients of a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r ≥ d`.
187	
188	Encoded as the functor sending an `S`-scheme `T → S` to the set of
189	equivalence classes `⟨F, q⟩` of pairs `(F, q)` with
190	`q : V_T ↠ F` a surjection of `O_T`-modules and `F` locally free of
191	rank `d`, modulo `ker(q) = ker(q')`. Concretely
192	`Grass(V, d) = Quot^{d, O_S}_{V/S/S}` (the Quot functor for `X = S`,
193	`E = V`, constant Hilbert polynomial `d`).
194	
195	iter-177+: the body re-exports `QuotFunctor (𝟙 S) (?) V Φ_d`, where
196	`Φ_d : Polynomial ℚ` is the constant polynomial `d`. For the iter-176
197	file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
198	noncomputable def Grassmannian {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
199	    (_V : S.Modules) (_d : ℕ) :
200	    (Over S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u :=
201	  sorry
202	
203	/-- **Representability of the Grassmannian.**
204	
205	For a noetherian scheme `S`, a locally free `O_S`-module `V` of rank `r`,
206	and `1 ≤ d ≤ r`, the Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d)` of
207	`Grassmannian` is representable by a smooth projective `S`-scheme
208	`Gr_S(V, d) ⟶ S` of relative dimension `d(r-d)`, equipped with a
209	tautological rank-`d` quotient `π* V ↠ U`. The determinant line bundle
210	`det(U)` is relatively very ample, giving a Plücker closed embedding
211	`Gr_S(V, d) ↪ ℙ_S(⋀^d V)`.
212	
213	We package the conclusion as the existence of a representing
214	`Y : Over S` together with a `Functor.RepresentableBy Y` witness for
215	`Grassmannian V d`; the additional projective / smooth / Plücker
216	structure is implicit in the construction and is iter-177+ refinement
217	work (once the proof body lands).
218	
219	iter-177+: the body follows Nitsure §1 "Construction of Grassmannian":
220	glue the `binom(r, d)` affine charts `U^I ≅ A^{d(r-d)}_S` along the
221	Plücker cocycle, verify separatedness via the diagonal cut, verify
222	properness by the DVR valuative criterion, build the tautological
223	quotient `U`, exhibit the Plücker embedding via the determinant line
224	bundle. For the iter-176 file-skeleton the body is a typed `sorry`. -/
225	theorem Grassmannian.representable {S : Scheme.{u}} [IsLocallyNoetherian S]
226	    (V : S.Modules) (d : ℕ) :
227	    ∃ (Y : Over S), Nonempty ((Grassmannian V d).RepresentableBy Y) := by
228	  sorry
229	
230	end Scheme

232	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — Quot/Grassmannian predicates
233	
234	These declarations build the support/freeness predicates of
235	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`, §"Support and freeness
236	predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no rank-`d` local
237	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme, so it is built here. -/
238	
239	namespace SheafOfModules
240	
241	/-- **Locally free of rank `d`** for a sheaf of modules on a scheme.
242	
243	A sheaf of modules `M` on a scheme `X` is *locally free of rank `d`* when `X`
244	admits an open cover `{U i}` on each member of which the restriction
245	`M|_{U i}` (the pullback of `M` along the open immersion `(U i).ι`) is
246	isomorphic to the free module of rank `d`, `O_{U i}^{⊕ d}` (encoded as
247	`SheafOfModules.free (ULift (Fin d))` over the structure-ring sheaf of the
248	open subscheme `(U i).toScheme`).
249	
250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
Now let me read the rest of QuotScheme.lean to understand it fully.
Let me now read the remainder of QuotScheme.lean and check FlatBaseChange.lean's sorry around line 1421.

250	This predicate is project-local: Mathlib does not supply a rank-indexed local
251	freeness predicate for sheaves of modules on a scheme. Blueprint:
252	`def:is_locally_free_of_rank` (Nitsure §1, Exercise (2)). -/
253	def IsLocallyFreeOfRank {X : Scheme.{u}} (M : X.Modules) (d : ℕ) : Prop :=
254	  ∃ (ι : Type u) (U : ι → X.Opens), (⨆ i, U i = ⊤) ∧
255	    ∀ i, Nonempty ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (U i).ι).obj M ≅
256	      _root_.SheafOfModules.free (R := (U i).toScheme.ringCatSheaf) (ULift.{u} (Fin d)))
257	
258	end SheafOfModules

260	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator ideal sheaf and schematic support
261	
262	These declarations build the annihilator ideal sheaf of a sheaf of modules and the
263	support/properness predicates of `blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`,
264	§"Support and freeness predicates". Mathlib (at the pinned commit) carries no
265	annihilator ideal sheaf for sheaves of modules on a scheme, nor a schematic-support
266	or proper-support predicate, so they are built here.
267	
268	The annihilator is packaged via `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, exactly mirroring
269	Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker` (which is `ofIdeals fun U ↦ RingHom.ker (f.app U).hom`):
270	`ofIdeals` produces *the largest ideal sheaf contained in* an arbitrary affine-local
271	family of ideals, so the structure's `map_ideal_basicOpen` coherence is discharged
272	internally and need not be supplied at definition time. The basic-open coherence that
273	makes the local annihilators agree with `ofIdeals` (the analogue of `Hom.ker_apply`,
274	`def:modules_annihilator`) is the separate characterization lemma `annihilator_ideal`,
275	which depends on the not-yet-closed QCoh→localization bridge
276	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen` (`lem:qcoh_section_localization_basicOpen`) together with
277	the algebra engine `Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map`
278	(`lem:annihilator_localization_eq_map`); see the handoff in
279	`task_results/.../QuotScheme.md`. -/
280	
281	namespace Scheme.Modules
282	
283	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
284	
285	/-- The **annihilator ideal sheaf** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
286	(`def:modules_annihilator`).
287	
288	On each affine open `U`, the intended section is the annihilator
289	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` of the `Γ(X,U)`-module of sections `Γ(F,U)`. The ideal sheaf
290	is assembled with `Scheme.IdealSheafData.ofIdeals`, the largest ideal sheaf contained
291	in that affine-local family — exactly the construction used for `Scheme.Hom.ker`. This
292	sidesteps proving the basic-open coherence (`map_ideal_basicOpen`) at definition time;
293	the identity `(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` is the downstream
294	characterization lemma (`annihilator_ideal`, blocked on the QCoh localization bridge).
295	
296	This is a project-local primitive: Mathlib does not carry an annihilator ideal sheaf
297	for sheaves of modules on a scheme. -/
298	noncomputable def annihilator (F : X.Modules) : X.IdealSheafData :=
299	  IdealSheafData.ofIdeals fun U => Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1)
300	
301	/-- The component of the annihilator ideal sheaf at an affine open is contained in the
302	module annihilator of the sections. This is the always-available (`ofIdeals`) direction
303	of the characterization; the reverse inclusion is the basic-open coherence blocked on
304	`isLocalizedModule_basicOpen`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
305	lemma annihilator_ideal_le (F : X.Modules) (U : X.affineOpens) :
306	    (annihilator F).ideal U ≤ Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) :=
307	  IdealSheafData.ideal_ofIdeals_le _ _
308	
309	/-- The **schematic support** of a sheaf of modules `F` on a scheme `X`
310	(`def:schematic_support`): the closed subscheme of `X` cut out by the annihilator
311	ideal sheaf `annihilator F`. Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
312	noncomputable def schematicSupport (F : X.Modules) : Scheme.{u} :=
313	  (annihilator F).subscheme
314	
315	/-- The canonical closed immersion of the schematic support into the ambient scheme,
316	realizing `schematicSupport F` as a closed subscheme of `X` (`def:schematic_support`).
317	This is the `IdealSheafData.subschemeι` of the annihilator ideal sheaf; it is a
318	`IsPreimmersion` + `QuasiCompact` immersion onto the support. Project-local because
319	`annihilator` is. -/
320	noncomputable def schematicSupportι (F : X.Modules) : schematicSupport F ⟶ X :=
321	  (annihilator F).subschemeι
322	
323	/-- The sheaf of modules `F` **has proper support over `S` along `f`**
324	(`def:has_proper_support`): the composite of the schematic-support immersion with
325	`f : X ⟶ S` is a proper morphism. Since `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper` is stable under
326	base change, this condition is preserved by pullback, as required by the Quot functor's
327	pullback action. Project-local because `schematicSupport` is. -/
328	def HasProperSupport {S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
329	  IsProper (schematicSupportι F ≫ f)
330	
331	end Scheme.Modules
332	
333	end AlgebraicGeometry
334	
335	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — annihilator under localization
336	
337	The annihilator ideal sheaf `def:modules_annihilator` of a coherent sheaf is
338	built from the affine-local data `U ↦ Ann_{O(U)}(F(U))`, packaged as a
339	`Scheme.IdealSheafData`. The structure's coherence field `map_ideal_basicOpen`
340	requires the algebraic fact that, for a *finitely generated* module, the
341	annihilator commutes with localization:
342	`Ann(S⁻¹M) = (Ann M)·S⁻¹R`. Mathlib (at the pinned commit) does not carry this
343	lemma, so it is supplied here as the load-bearing engine for that construction.
344	-/
345	
346	namespace Module
347	
348	/-- For a finitely generated module `M` over a commutative ring `R`, the
349	annihilator commutes with localization: if `Rₚ` localizes `R` at a submonoid
350	`S` and `f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ` localizes `M` at `S`, then the annihilator of `Mₚ`
351	over `Rₚ` is the extension (`Ideal.map` along `algebraMap R Rₚ`) of the
352	annihilator of `M` over `R`.
353	
354	This is the abstract `IsLocalization`/`IsLocalizedModule` form, matching the
355	shape needed for the affine-basic-open coherence of the annihilator ideal sheaf
356	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.annihilator`, `def:modules_annihilator`):
357	the structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(X,U) → Γ(X, D(f))` is
358	`IsLocalization (powers f)`, and for a quasi-coherent `F` the section
359	restriction is `IsLocalizedModule (powers f)`.
360	
361	Mathlib has no annihilator-localization lemma, so this is project-local. -/
362	theorem annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map
363	    {R : Type*} [CommRing R] (S : Submonoid R)
364	    {Rₚ : Type*} [CommRing Rₚ] [Algebra R Rₚ] [IsLocalization S Rₚ]
365	    {M : Type*} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [Module.Finite R M]
366	    {Mₚ : Type*} [AddCommGroup Mₚ] [Module R Mₚ] [Module Rₚ Mₚ] [IsScalarTower R Rₚ Mₚ]
367	    (f : M →ₗ[R] Mₚ) [IsLocalizedModule S f] :
368	    Module.annihilator Rₚ Mₚ = (Module.annihilator R M).map (algebraMap R Rₚ) := by
369	  classical
370	  obtain ⟨t, htop⟩ := (Module.Finite.fg_top (R := R) (M := M))
371	  -- annihilating a spanning finset suffices for membership in the annihilator
372	  have key : ∀ (r : R), (∀ m ∈ t, r • m = 0) → r ∈ Module.annihilator R M := by
373	    intro r h
374	    rw [Module.mem_annihilator]
375	    intro x
376	    have hx : x ∈ Submodule.span R (t : Set M) := htop ▸ Submodule.mem_top
377	    induction hx using Submodule.span_induction with
378	    | mem y hy => exact h y hy
379	    | zero => simp
380	    | add a b _ _ ha hb => rw [smul_add, ha, hb, add_zero]

967	The LHS unwinding of `pushforwardBaseChangeMap` on global sections splits at three categorical

977	-- The conjugate-unit calculus chains several `erw` defeq-unifications and a `simp` closure over the

980	/-- **Seam 1: the affine pullback–pushforward unit is the algebraic unit.** Let
981	`ι_A : A → A ⊗_R R'` be the canonical inclusion. The unit of the
982	`((Spec ι_A)^*, (Spec ι_A)_*)`-adjunction evaluated at `tilde M`, read on global sections over
